# NautilusTrader Documentation

[Welcome to NautilusTrader's Documentation](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/#welcome-to-nautilustraders-documentation)

=============================================================================================================================

NautilusTrader is an open-source, production-grade, Rust-native engine for multi-asset, multi-venue trading systems.

The system spans research, deterministic simulation, and live execution within a single event-driven architecture, with Python serving as the control plane for strategy logic, configuration, and orchestration.

This separation provides the performance and safety of a compiled trading engine with the flexibility of Python for system composition and strategy development. Trading systems can also be written entirely in Rust for mission-critical workloads.

The same execution semantics and deterministic time model operate in both research and live systems. Strategies deploy from research to production with no code changes, providing research-to-live parity and reducing the divergence that typically introduces deployment risk.

NautilusTrader is asset-class-agnostic. Any venue with a REST API or WebSocket feed can be integrated through modular adapters. Current integrations span crypto exchanges (CEX and DEX), traditional markets (FX, equities, futures, options), and betting exchanges.

[### Getting Started\
\
Installation guide and tutorials for setting up and running your first backtests.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started)
[### Concepts\
\
Core architecture, components, and design principles behind the trading engine.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/concepts)
[### Tutorials\
\
Step-by-step walkthroughs for backtesting and live trading strategies.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tutorials)
[### Integrations\
\
Adapter integrations for connecting with trading venues and data providers.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/integrations)
[### Developer Guide\
\
Guides for contributing, extending, and developing the trading engine.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/developer_guide)

[Getting Started\
\
Next Page](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started/)

### On this page

[Welcome to NautilusTrader's Documentation](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/#welcome-to-nautilustraders-documentation)

NautilusTrader Python API[¶](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/#nautilustrader-python-api "Link to this heading")

=================================================================================================================================

NautilusTrader is an open-source, production-grade, Rust-native engine for multi-asset, multi-venue trading systems.

The system spans research, deterministic simulation, and live execution within a single event-driven architecture, with Python serving as the control plane for strategy logic, configuration, and orchestration.

This separation provides the performance and safety of a compiled trading engine with the flexibility of Python for system composition and strategy development. Trading systems can also be written entirely in Rust for mission-critical workloads.

The same execution semantics and deterministic time model operate in both research and live systems. Strategies deploy from research to production with no code changes, providing research-to-live parity and reducing the divergence that typically introduces deployment risk.

NautilusTrader is asset-class-agnostic. Any venue with a REST API or WebSocket feed can be integrated through modular adapters. Current integrations span crypto exchanges (CEX and DEX), traditional markets (FX, equities, futures, options), and betting exchanges.

This reference covers every public module, class, method, and function in the Python framework.

Modules[¶](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/#modules "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Module | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Accounting](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/accounting.html) | Account management and balance tracking |
| [Adapters](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/adapters/index.html) | Exchange and broker integrations |
| [Analysis](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/analysis.html) | Performance analysis and statistics |
| [Backtest](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/backtest.html) | Historical backtesting engine |
| [Cache](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/cache.html) | In-memory data and state caching |
| [Common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/common.html) | Shared components, clocks, loggers, and factories |
| [Config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/config.html) | Configuration objects for system components |
| [Core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/core.html) | Core primitives, datetime utilities, and UUID types |
| [Data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/data.html) | Data engine, clients, and aggregation |
| [Execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/execution.html) | Execution engine, clients, and algorithms |
| [Indicators](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/indicators.html) | Technical analysis indicators |
| [Live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/live.html) | Live trading engine and execution |
| [Model](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/model/index.html) | Domain model: instruments, orders, events, and data |
| [Persistence](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/persistence.html) | Data persistence and catalog |
| [Portfolio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/portfolio.html) | Portfolio management |
| [Risk](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/risk.html) | Risk engine and management |
| [Serialization](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/serialization.html) | Message serialization |
| [System](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/system.html) | System kernel and process management |
| [Trading](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/python-api-latest/trading.html) | Trading strategies and controllers |

# NautilusTrader Documentation

Getting Started

[Getting Started](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#getting-started)

=========================================================================================

To get started with NautilusTrader you need:

*   Python 3.12–3.14 with the `nautilus_trader` package installed
*   A way to run Python scripts or Jupyter notebooks

[### Installation\
\
Install NautilusTrader on your machine.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/installation)
[### Quickstart\
\
Set up and run your first backtest.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/quickstart)

[Examples](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#examples)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

The docs cover a subset of examples. For the full set, see the [nautilus\_trader repository](https://github.com/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader)
.

| Directory | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [examples/](https://github.com/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader/tree/develop/examples) | Self-contained runnable Python examples |
| [docs/tutorials/](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/tutorials/) | Jupyter notebook tutorials for common workflows |
| [docs/concepts/](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/concepts/) | Concept guides with code snippets |
| [nautilus\_trader/examples/](https://github.com/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader/tree/master/nautilus_trader/examples) | Basic strategies, indicators, and execution algorithms |
| [tests/unit\_tests/](https://github.com/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader/tree/master/tests/unit_tests) | Unit tests covering core functionality |

[Backtesting API levels](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#backtesting-api-levels)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NautilusTrader provides two API levels for backtesting:

| API Level | Description | Characteristics |
| --- | --- | --- |
| High-Level | `BacktestNode` / `TradingNode` | Recommended for production, easier transition to live trading; requires a Parquet data catalog |
| Low-Level | `BacktestEngine` | For library development, direct component access; no live-trading path |

One node per process

Running multiple `BacktestNode` or `TradingNode` instances in the same process is not supported due to global singleton state. Sequential execution with proper disposal between runs is supported. See [Processes and threads](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/concepts/architecture#processes-and-threads)
.

See the [Backtesting](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/concepts/backtesting)
 guide for help choosing an API level.

[### Backtest (low-level API)\
\
Load raw data with loaders and wranglers, then run a backtest with BacktestEngine.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/backtest_low_level)
[### Backtest (high-level API)\
\
Load raw data into the data catalog, then run a backtest with BacktestNode.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/backtest_high_level)

[Running in Docker](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#running-in-docker)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A self-contained Jupyter notebook server is available as a Docker image, no local setup required.

    docker pull ghcr.io/nautechsystems/jupyterlab:nightly --platform linux/amd64
    docker run -p 8888:8888 ghcr.io/nautechsystems/jupyterlab:nightly

Then open `http://localhost:8888` in your browser.

Container data is ephemeral, deleting the container removes all data.

NautilusTrader log output can exceed Jupyter's default rate limit, causing notebooks to hang. Set `log_level` to `"ERROR"` to avoid this.

[NautilusTrader Documentation\
\
Previous Page](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/)
[Installation\
\
Next Page](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started/installation/)

[Getting Started](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#getting-started)
[Examples](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#examples)
[Backtesting API levels](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/getting_started#backtesting-api-levels)

# NautilusTrader Documentation

Tutorials

[Tutorials](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tutorials#tutorials)

=======================================================================

Step-by-step walkthroughs demonstrating specific features and workflows.

Each tutorial is generated from a Jupyter notebook located in the docs [tutorials directory](https://github.com/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader/tree/develop/docs/tutorials)
. These notebooks serve as valuable learning aids and let you execute the code interactively.

*   Make sure you are using the tutorial docs that match your NautilusTrader version:
*   **Latest**: These docs are built from the HEAD of the `master` branch and work with the latest stable release. See [https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tutorials/](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tutorials/)
    .
*   **Nightly**: These docs are built from the HEAD of the `nightly` branch and work with bleeding-edge and experimental features. See [https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/tutorials/](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/tutorials/)
    .

[Running in docker](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tutorials#running-in-docker)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Alternatively, a self-contained dockerized Jupyter notebook server is available for download, which requires no setup or installation. This is the fastest way to get up and running to try out NautilusTrader. Note that deleting the container will also delete any data.

*   To get started, install docker:
    *   Go to [Docker installation guide](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/)
         and follow the instructions.
*   From a terminal, download the latest image:
    *   `docker pull ghcr.io/nautechsystems/jupyterlab:nightly --platform linux/amd64`
*   Run the docker container, exposing the Jupyter port:
    *   `docker run -p 8888:8888 ghcr.io/nautechsystems/jupyterlab:nightly`
*   When the container starts, a URL with an access token will be printed in the terminal. Copy that URL and open it in your browser, for example:
    *   [http://localhost:8888](http://localhost:8888/)
        

Examples use `log_level="ERROR"` because Nautilus logging exceeds Jupyter's stdout rate limit, causing notebooks to hang at lower log levels.

[Order Book\
\
Previous Page](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/concepts/order_book/)

# NautilusTrader Documentation

Concepts

[Concepts](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/concepts#concepts)

====================================================================

These guides explain the core components, architecture, and design of NautilusTrader.

[### Overview\
\
Main features and intended use cases for the platform.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/overview)
[### Architecture\
\
The principles, structures, and designs that underpin the platform.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/architecture)
[### Actors\
\
The Actor component for interacting with the trading system.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/actors)
[### Strategies\
\
How to implement trading strategies using the Strategy component.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/strategies)
[### Instruments\
\
Instrument definitions for tradable assets and contracts.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/instruments)
[### Value Types\
\
Immutable numeric types (Price, Quantity, Money), their arithmetic behavior, precision handling, and type-specific constraints.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/value_types)
[### Data\
\
Built-in data types for the trading domain, and how to work with custom data.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/data)
[### Execution\
\
Trade execution and order management across multiple strategies and venues, including components and the flow of execution messages.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/execution)
[### Orders\
\
Available order types, supported execution instructions, advanced order types, and emulated orders.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/orders)
[### Positions\
\
Position lifecycle, aggregation from order fills, PnL calculations, and position snapshotting for netting OMS configurations.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/positions)
[### Cache\
\
The Cache is the central in-memory store for all trading-related data.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/cache)
[### Message Bus\
\
Decoupled messaging between components, supporting point-to-point, publish/subscribe, and request/response patterns.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/message_bus)
[### Portfolio\
\
Tracks all positions across strategies and instruments, providing a unified view of holdings, risk exposure, and performance.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/portfolio)
[### Reports\
\
Execution reports, portfolio analysis, PnL accounting, and backtest post-run analysis.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/reports)
[### Logging\
\
High-performance logging for both backtesting and live trading, implemented in Rust.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/logging)
[### Backtesting\
\
Running simulated trading on historical data using a specific system implementation.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/backtesting)
[### Visualization\
\
Interactive tearsheets for analyzing backtest results, including charts, themes, customization options, and custom visualizations.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/visualization)
[### Live Trading\
\
Deploying backtested strategies in real-time without code changes, and the key differences between backtesting and live trading.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/live)
[### Adapters\
\
Requirements and best practices for developing integration adapters for data providers and trading venues.](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/adapters)

The Python API reference (linked in the sidebar) is the source of truth for the platform. If there are discrepancies between these guides and the API reference, the API reference is correct.

# NautilusTrader Documentation

Integrations

[Integrations](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/integrations#integrations)

================================================================================

NautilusTrader uses modular _adapters_ to connect to trading venues and data providers, translating raw APIs into a unified interface and normalized domain model.

The following integrations are currently supported:

| Name | ID  | Type | Status | Docs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [AX Exchange](https://architect.exchange/) | `AX` | Perpetuals Exchange | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/beta-yellow) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/architect_ax) |
| [Architect](https://architect.co/) | `ARCHITECT` | Brokerage (multi-venue) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/planned-gray) | \-  |
| [Betfair](https://betfair.com/) | `BETFAIR` | Sports Betting Exchange | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/betfair) |
| [Binance](https://binance.com/) | `BINANCE` | Crypto Exchange (CEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/binance) |
| [BitMEX](https://www.bitmex.com/) | `BITMEX` | Crypto Exchange (CEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/bitmex) |
| [Bybit](https://www.bybit.com/) | `BYBIT` | Crypto Exchange (CEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/bybit) |
| [Databento](https://databento.com/) | `DATABENTO` | Data Provider | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/databento) |
| [Deribit](https://www.deribit.com/) | `DERIBIT` | Crypto Exchange (CEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/beta-yellow) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/deribit) |
| [dYdX](https://dydx.exchange/) | `DYDX` | Crypto Exchange (DEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/beta-yellow) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/dydx) |
| [Hyperliquid](https://hyperliquid.xyz/) | `HYPERLIQUID` | Crypto Exchange (DEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/beta-yellow) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/hyperliquid) |
| [Interactive Brokers](https://www.interactivebrokers.com/) | `INTERACTIVE_BROKERS` | Brokerage (multi-venue) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/ib) |
| [Kraken](https://kraken.com/) | `KRAKEN` | Crypto Exchange (CEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/beta-yellow) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/kraken) |
| [OKX](https://okx.com/) | `OKX` | Crypto Exchange (CEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/okx) |
| [Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/) | `POLYMARKET` | Prediction Market (DEX) | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/polymarket) |
| [Tardis](https://tardis.dev/) | `TARDIS` | Crypto Data Provider | ![status](https://img.shields.io/badge/stable-green) | [Guide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tardis) |

*   **ID**: The default client ID for the integrations adapter clients.
*   **Type**: The type of integration (often the venue type).

[Status](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/integrations#status)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `planned`: Planned for future development.
*   `building`: Under construction and likely not in a usable state.
*   `beta`: Completed to a minimally working state and in a 'beta' testing phase.
*   `stable`: Stabilized feature set and API, the integration has been tested by both developers and users to a reasonable level (some bugs may still remain).

[Implementation goals](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/integrations#implementation-goals)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The primary goal of NautilusTrader is to provide a unified trading system for use with a variety of integrations. To support the widest range of trading strategies, priority will be given to _standard_ functionality:

*   Requesting historical market data.
*   Streaming live market data.
*   Reconciling execution state.
*   Submitting standard order types with standard execution instructions.
*   Modifying existing orders (if possible on an exchange).
*   Canceling orders.

The implementation of each integration aims to meet the following criteria:

*   Low-level client components should match the exchange API as closely as possible.
*   The full range of an exchange's functionality (where applicable to NautilusTrader) should _eventually_ be supported.
*   Exchange specific data types will be added to support the functionality and return types which are reasonably expected by a user.
*   Actions unsupported by an exchange or NautilusTrader will be logged as a warning or error when invoked.

[API unification](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/integrations#api-unification)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All integrations must conform to NautilusTrader’s system API, requiring normalization and standardization:

*   Symbols should use the venue’s native symbol format unless disambiguation is required (e.g., Binance Spot vs. Binance Futures).
*   Timestamps must use UNIX epoch nanoseconds. If milliseconds are used, field/property names should explicitly end with `_ms`.

[Loading External Data\
\
Previous Page](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/tutorials/loading_external_data/)

\
Next Page](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/latest/integrations/architect_ax/)

-----------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_trader/all.html "show sidebar")

Rustdoc help
============

[Back](javascript:void(0))

Keyboard Shortcuts
------------------

?

Show this help dialog

S / /

Focus the search field

↑

Move up in search results

↓

Move down in search results

← / →

Switch result tab (when results focused)

⏎

Go to active search result

+ / \=

Expand all sections

\-

Collapse all sections

\_

Collapse all sections, including impl blocks

Search Tricks
-------------

For a full list of all search features, take a look [here](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/rustdoc/read-documentation/search.html)
.

Prefix searches with a type followed by a colon (e.g., `fn:`) to restrict the search to a given item kind.

Accepted kinds are: `fn`, `mod`, `struct`, `enum`, `trait`, `type`, `macro`, and `constant`.

Search functions by type signature (e.g., `vec -> usize` or `-> vec` or `String, enum:Cow -> bool`)

You can look for items with an exact name by putting double quotes around your request: `"string"`

Look for functions that accept or return [slices](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.slice.html)
 and [arrays](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.array.html)
 by writing square brackets (e.g., `-> [u8]` or `[] -> Option`)

Look for items inside another one by searching for a path: `vec::Vec`

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/all.html "show sidebar")

Crate nautilus\_analysis Copy item path
=======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_analysis/lib.rs.html#16-70)

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

Portfolio analysis and performance metrics for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-analysis` crate provides a comprehensive suite of portfolio analysis tools and performance statistics for evaluating trading strategies and portfolios. This includes return-based metrics, PnL-based statistics, and risk measurements commonly used in quantitative finance:

*   Portfolio analyzer for tracking account states and positions.
*   Extensive collection of performance statistics and risk metrics.
*   Flexible statistic calculation framework supporting different data sources.
*   Support for multi-currency portfolios and unrealized PnL calculations.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/index.html#platform)
Platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[analyzer](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/analyzer/index.html "mod nautilus_analysis::analyzer")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/python/index.html "mod nautilus_analysis::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[statistic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/statistic/index.html "mod nautilus_analysis::statistic")

[statistics](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/statistics/index.html "mod nautilus_analysis::statistics")

Trading performance statistics and portfolio metrics.

Type Aliases[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/index.html#types)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Returns](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_analysis/type.Returns.html "type nautilus_analysis::Returns")

Type alias for time-indexed returns data used in portfolio analysis.

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for Ax’s [AX Exchange](https://architect.exchange/)
.

[AX Exchange](https://architect.exchange/)
 is the world’s first centralized and regulated exchange for perpetual futures on traditional underlying asset classes (FX, rates, metals, energy, stock indexes). Designed for institutional and professional traders, it combines innovations from digital asset perpetual exchanges with the safety and risk management of traditional futures exchanges. Licensed under the [Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA)](https://www.bma.bm/)
.

The `nautilus-architect-ax` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models, and helper utilities that wrap the official AX Exchange API.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation:

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings via [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used together with `python`).

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#documentation)
Documentation
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   API reference: [https://docs.architect.exchange/api-reference/](https://docs.architect.exchange/api-reference/)
    
*   Crate docs: [https://docs.rs/nautilus-architect-ax](https://docs.rs/nautilus-architect-ax)
    

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/common/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::common")

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/config/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::config")

Configuration structures for the AX Exchange adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the AX Exchange adapter.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::factories")

Factory functions for creating AX Exchange clients and components.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_architect_ax/http/index.html "mod nautilus_architect_ax::http")

HTTP REST API client for Ax.

Summary

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Betfair](https://www.betfair.com/)
 betting exchange.

The `nautilus-betfair` crate provides data and execution clients, streaming and REST API models, and full NautilusTrader integration for the Betfair betting exchange.

The official API reference can be found at [https://docs.developer.betfair.com/](https://docs.developer.betfair.com/)
.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_betfair/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_betfair/index.html#naming-conventions)
Naming conventions
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Betfair’s API uses British English spelling. This crate preserves those spellings in type names, method strings, and fixture files, e.g. `MarketCatalogue` / `listMarketCatalogue`, not “catalog”.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_betfair/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `high-precision`: Enables 128-bit value types from `nautilus-model`.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_betfair/index.html#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# nautilus_backtest - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html#main-content)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

[Crate nautilus\_backtest](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/all.html "show sidebar")

Crate nautilus\_backtest Copy item path
=======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/lib.rs.html#16-77)

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

Backtest engine for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-backtest` crate provides a comprehensive event-driven backtesting framework that allows quantitative traders to test and validate trading strategies on historical data with high fidelity market simulation. The system replicates real market conditions including:

*   Event-driven backtesting engine with simulated exchanges.
*   Market data replay with configurable latency and fill models.
*   Order matching engines with realistic execution simulation.
*   Multi-venue and multi-asset backtesting capabilities.
*   Comprehensive configuration and state management.
*   Integration with live trading systems for seamless deployment.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html#platform)
Platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `examples`: Enables example strategies and the EMA crossover backtest example.
*   `streaming`: Enables `persistence` dependency for streaming configuration.
*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[accumulator](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/accumulator/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::accumulator")

Time event accumulation and scheduling for the backtest engine.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/config/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::config")

Configuration types for the backtest engine, venues, data, and run parameters.

[data\_client](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/data_client/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::data_client")

Provides a `BacktestDataClient` implementation for backtesting.

[data\_iterator](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/data_iterator/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::data_iterator")

Multi-stream, time-ordered data iterator for replaying historical market data.

[engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::engine")

The core `BacktestEngine` for backtesting on historical data.

[exchange](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/exchange/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::exchange")

Provides a `SimulatedExchange` venue for backtesting on historical data.

[execution\_client](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/execution_client/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::execution_client")

Provides a `BacktestExecutionClient` implementation for backtesting.

[ffi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/ffi/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::ffi")

C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
.

[modules](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/modules/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::modules")

Simulation module trait for extending backtesting with custom venue behaviors.

[node](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::node")

Provides a [`BacktestNode`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html "struct nautilus_backtest::node::BacktestNode")
 that orchestrates catalog-driven backtests.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/python/index.html "mod nautilus_backtest::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Binance](https://www.binance.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-binance` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models, and helper utilities that wrap the official **Binance API**, covering:

*   Spot trading (api.binance.com)
*   Spot margin trading
*   USD-M Futures (fapi.binance.com)
*   COIN-M Futures (dapi.binance.com)
*   European Options (eapi.binance.com)

The official Binance API reference can be found at [https://binance-docs.github.io/apidocs/](https://binance-docs.github.io/apidocs/)
.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case (Rust-only builds vs. Python bindings through PyO3).

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings via [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used together with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html#documentation)
Documentation
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [https://docs.rs/nautilus-binance](https://docs.rs/nautilus-binance)
 for the latest API documentation.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/common/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::common")

Common types, constants, and utilities for the Binance adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/config/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::config")

Binance adapter configuration structures.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::factories")

Factory functions for creating Binance clients and components.

[futures](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::futures")

Binance Futures adapter components.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/python/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::python")

Python bindings for the Binance adapter.

[spot](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/spot/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::spot")

Binance Spot market adapter with full SBE (Simple Binary Encoding) support.

Summary

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [BitMEX](https://bitmex.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-bitmex` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models and helper utilities that wrap the official **BitMEX API**.

The official BitMEX API reference can be found at [https://www.bitmex.com/app/apiOverview](https://www.bitmex.com/app/apiOverview)
. All public links inside this crate reference the official documentation.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bitmex/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .

Expand description

Command-line interface and tools for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-cli` crate provides a comprehensive command-line interface for managing and operating NautilusTrader installations. It includes tools for database management, system configuration, and operational utilities:

*   Database initialization and management commands.
*   PostgreSQL schema setup and maintenance.
*   Configuration validation and setup utilities.
*   System administration and operational tools.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cli/index.html#platform)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cli/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case:

*   `defi`: Enables DeFi functionality including blockchain data access and pool analysis.

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_blockchain/index.html#main-content)

=========================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_blockchain/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_blockchain/lib.rs.html#16-81)

Expand description

Blockchain data adapter for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-blockchain` crate provides a high-performance, universal, extensible adapter for ingesting DeFi data from decentralized exchanges (DEXs), liquidity pools, and on-chain events. It enables you to power analytics pipelines and trading strategies with real-time and historical on-chain data.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_blockchain/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_blockchain/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `hypersync`: Enables the [HyperSync](https://envio.dev/#hypersync)
     client integration.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).
*   `turmoil`: Enables deterministic network simulation testing with [turmoil](https://github.com/tokio-rs/turmoil)
    .

# nautilus_bybit - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_bybit](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_bybit Copy item path
====================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_bybit/lib.rs.html#16-68)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Bybit](https://www.bybit.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-bybit` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models, and helper utilities that wrap the official **Bybit v5 API**.

The official Bybit API reference can be found at [https://bybit-exchange.github.io/docs/v5/intro](https://bybit-exchange.github.io/docs/v5/intro)
. All public links inside this crate reference the English version of the documentation.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case (Rust-only builds vs. Python bindings through PyO3).

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings via [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used together with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html#documentation)
Documentation
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [https://docs.rs/nautilus-bybit](https://docs.rs/nautilus-bybit)
 for the latest API documentation.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html#modules)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/common/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::common")

Shared primitives and utilities for the Bybit adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/config/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::config")

Configuration structures for the Bybit adapter.

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/data/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::data")

Live market data client implementation for the Bybit adapter.

[error](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::error")

Unified error handling for the Bybit adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the Bybit adapter.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::factories")

Factory functions for creating Bybit clients and components.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/http/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::http")

HTTP client bindings for the Bybit adapter.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/python/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::python")

Python bindings from `pyo3`.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_bybit::websocket")

WebSocket client bindings for the Bybit adapter.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_core Copy item path
===================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/lib.rs.html#16-109)

Expand description

Core foundational types and utilities for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-core` crate is designed to be lightweight, efficient, and to provide zero-cost abstractions wherever possible. It supplies the essential building blocks used across the NautilusTrader ecosystem, including:

*   Time handling and atomic clock functionality.
*   UUID generation and management.
*   Mathematical functions and interpolation utilities.
*   Correctness validation functions.
*   Serialization traits and helpers.
*   Cross-platform environment utilities.
*   Abstractions over common collections.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html#reexports)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::params::[from_pydict](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/params/fn.from_pydict.html "fn nautilus_core::params::from_pydict") ;`

`pub use crate::drop::[CleanDrop](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/drop/trait.CleanDrop.html "trait nautilus_core::drop::CleanDrop") ;`

`pub use crate::nanos::[UnixNanos](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/struct.UnixNanos.html "struct nautilus_core::nanos::UnixNanos") ;`

`pub use crate::params::[Params](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/params/struct.Params.html "struct nautilus_core::params::Params") ;`

`pub use crate::shared::[SharedCell](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/shared/struct.SharedCell.html "struct nautilus_core::shared::SharedCell") ;`

`pub use crate::shared::[WeakCell](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/shared/struct.WeakCell.html "struct nautilus_core::shared::WeakCell") ;`

`pub use crate::stack_str::[STACKSTR_CAPACITY](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/constant.STACKSTR_CAPACITY.html "constant nautilus_core::stack_str::STACKSTR_CAPACITY") ;`

`pub use crate::stack_str::[StackStr](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html "struct nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr") ;`

`pub use crate::time::[AtomicTime](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/struct.AtomicTime.html "struct nautilus_core::time::AtomicTime") ;`

`pub use crate::uuid::[UUID4](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/uuid/struct.UUID4.html "struct nautilus_core::uuid::UUID4") ;`

The core `AtomicTime` for real-time and static clocks.

[uuid](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/uuid/index.html "mod nautilus_core::uuid")

=====================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_common/lib.rs.html#16-91)

Expand description

Common componentry for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-common` crate provides shared components and utilities that form the system foundation for NautilusTrader applications. This includes the actor system, message bus, caching layer, and other essential services.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.
*   `indicators`: Includes the `nautilus-indicators` crate and indicator utilities.
*   `capnp`: Enables [Cap’n Proto](https://capnproto.org/)
     serialization support.
*   `live`: Enables the Tokio async runtime for live trading.
*   `tracing-bridge`: Enables the `tracing` subscriber bridge for log integration.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[actor](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/actor/index.html "mod nautilus_common::actor")

Actor system for event-driven message processing.

[cache](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/cache/index.html "mod nautilus_common::cache")

In-memory cache for market and execution data, with optional persistent backing.

[clients](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/clients/index.html "mod nautilus_common::clients")

Client trait definitions for data and execution clients.

[clock](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/clock/index.html "mod nautilus_common::clock")

Real-time and static `Clock` implementations.

[component](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/component/index.html "mod nautilus_common::component")

Component system for managing stateful system entities.

A user custom data type.

DeFi (Decentralized Finance) integration for NautilusTrader.

[enums](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/enums/index.html "mod nautilus_common::enums")

Enumerations for common components.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_common::factories")

Factories for constructing domain objects such as orders and events.

[ffi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/ffi/index.html "mod nautilus_common::ffi")

C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
.

[generators](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/generators/index.html "mod nautilus_common::generators")

Provides generation of identifiers such as `ClientOrderId` and `PositionId`.

[greeks](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/greeks/index.html "mod nautilus_common::greeks")

Greeks calculator for options and futures.

[live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/live/index.html "mod nautilus_common::live")

Live (async/tokio) components for real-time trading.

[logging](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/logging/index.html "mod nautilus_common::logging")

The logging framework for Nautilus systems.

[messages](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/messages/index.html "mod nautilus_common::messages")

Message types for system communication.

[msgbus](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/msgbus/index.html "mod nautilus_common::msgbus")

In-memory message bus for intra-process communication.

[providers](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/providers/index.html "mod nautilus_common::providers")

Instrument provider trait and shared instrument storage.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/python/index.html "mod nautilus_common::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[runner](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/runner/index.html "mod nautilus_common::runner")

Global runtime machinery and thread-local storage.

[serialization](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/serialization/index.html "mod nautilus_common::serialization")

Serialization implementations for Nautilus common types.

[signal](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/signal/index.html "mod nautilus_common::signal")

A user signal type.

[testing](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/testing/index.html "mod nautilus_common::testing")

Common test related helper functions.

[throttler](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/throttler/index.html "mod nautilus_common::throttler")

Message throttling and rate limiting functionality.

[timer](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/timer/index.html "mod nautilus_common::timer")

Real-time and test timers for use with `Clock` implementations.

[xrate](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/xrate/index.html "mod nautilus_common::xrate")

Exchange rate calculations between currencies.

Macros[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html#macros)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[log\_debug](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_debug.html "macro nautilus_common::log_debug")

Logs a debug message with automatic color mapping or custom color and component.

[log\_error](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_error.html "macro nautilus_common::log_error")

Logs an error message with automatic red color or custom color and component.

[log\_info](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_info.html "macro nautilus_common::log_info")

Logs an info message with automatic color mapping or custom color and component.

[log\_trace](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_trace.html "macro nautilus_common::log_trace")

Logs a trace message with automatic color mapping or custom color and component.

[log\_warn](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_warn.html "macro nautilus_common::log_warn")

Logs a warning message with automatic yellow color or custom color and component.

# nautilus_cryptography - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#main-content)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

[Crate nautilus\_cryptography](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_cryptography Copy item path
===========================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_cryptography/lib.rs.html#16-62)

Expand description

Cryptographic utilities and security functions for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-cryptography` crate provides essential cryptographic primitives and security utilities required for secure communication with trading venues and data providers. This includes digital signing, TLS configuration, and cryptographic provider management:

*   HMAC-based message authentication and signing.
*   Digital signatures using RSA and Ed25519 algorithms.
*   TLS client configuration with platform certificate verification.
*   Cryptographic provider management and initialization.
*   Secure encoding and decoding utilities.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[providers](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/providers/index.html "mod nautilus_cryptography::providers")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/python/index.html "mod nautilus_cryptography::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[signing](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/signing/index.html "mod nautilus_cryptography::signing")

[tls](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/tls/index.html "mod nautilus_cryptography::tls")

# nautilus_data - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#main-content)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_data Copy item path
===================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_data/lib.rs.html#16-68)

Expand description

Data engine and market data processing for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-data` crate provides a comprehensive framework for handling market data ingestion, processing, and aggregation within the NautilusTrader ecosystem. This includes real-time data streaming, historical data management, and various aggregation methodologies:

*   High-performance data engine for orchestrating data operations.
*   Data client infrastructure for connecting to market data providers.
*   Bar aggregation machinery supporting tick, volume, value, and time-based aggregation.
*   Order book management and delta processing capabilities.
*   Subscription management and data request handling.
*   Configurable data routing and processing pipelines.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `streaming`: Enables `persistence` dependency for catalog-based data streaming.
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#reexports)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use client::[DataClientAdapter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/client/struct.DataClientAdapter.html "struct nautilus_data::client::DataClientAdapter") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[aggregation](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/aggregation/index.html "mod nautilus_data::aggregation")

Bar aggregation machinery.

[client](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/client/index.html "mod nautilus_data::client")

Base data client functionality.

[defi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/index.html "mod nautilus_data::defi")

DeFi (Decentralized Finance) integration for the data crate.

[engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/engine/index.html "mod nautilus_data::engine")

Provides a high-performance `DataEngine` for all environments.

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html?search=)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_databento/lib.rs.html#16-67)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for [Databento](https://databento.com/)
.

The `nautilus-databento` crate provides a complete integration with the Databento API for accessing institutional-grade market data feeds across multiple venues and asset classes.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#platform)
Platform
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case:

*   `live` (default): Enables live data functionality including the `data`, `factories`, and `live` modules.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/common/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::common")

Common functions to support Databento adapter operations.

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/data/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::data")

Provides a unified data client that combines Databento’s live streaming and historical data capabilities.

[decode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/decode/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::decode")

Databento message decoding functions.

[enums](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/enums/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::enums")

Enumerations for the Databento integration.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::factories")

Factory functions for creating Databento clients and components.

[historical](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_databento/historical/index.html "mod nautilus_databento::historical")

Core Databento historical client for both Rust and Python usage.

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html?search=)
