Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_deribit/lib.rs.html#16-65)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Deribit](https://www.deribit.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-deribit` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models and helper utilities that wrap the official **Deribit API v2**.

Deribit uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over both HTTP and WebSocket transports (not REST). WebSocket is preferred for subscriptions and real-time data.

The official Deribit API reference can be found at [https://docs.deribit.com/v2/](https://docs.deribit.com/v2/)
.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/index.html#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/common/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::common")

Common types and utilities for the Deribit adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/config/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::config")

Configuration structures for the Deribit adapter.

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/data/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::data")

Live market data client implementation for the Deribit adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the Deribit adapter.

[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/factories/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::factories")

Factory functions for creating Deribit clients and components.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/http/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::http")

Deribit HTTP client implementation using JSON-RPC 2.0.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/python/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::python")

Python bindings from `pyo3`.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_deribit/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_deribit::websocket")

Deribit WebSocket client implementation using JSON-RPC 2.0.

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .

# nautilus_execution - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#main-content)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

[Crate nautilus\_execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_execution Copy item path
========================================

[Search](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html?search=)

[Settings](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/settings.html)

[Help](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/help.html)

Summary[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_execution/lib.rs.html#16-76)

Expand description

Order execution system for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-execution` crate provides a comprehensive order execution system that handles the complete order lifecycle from submission to fill processing. This includes sophisticated order matching, execution venue integration, and advanced order type emulation:

*   **Execution engine**: Central orchestration of order routing and position management.
*   **Order matching engine**: High-fidelity market simulation for backtesting and paper trading.
*   **Order emulator**: Advanced order types not natively supported by venues (trailing stops, contingent orders).
*   **Execution clients**: Abstract interfaces for connecting to trading venues and brokers.
*   **Order manager**: Local order lifecycle management and state tracking.
*   **Matching core**: Low-level order book and price-time priority matching algorithms.
*   **Fee and fill models**: Configurable execution cost simulation and realistic fill behavior.

The crate supports both live trading environments (with real execution clients) and simulated environments (with matching engines), making it suitable for production trading, strategy development, and comprehensive backtesting.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#platform)
Platform
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[client](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/client/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::client")

Execution client implementations for trading venue connectivity.

[engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/engine/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::engine")

Provides a generic `ExecutionEngine` for all environments.

[matching\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/matching_core/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::matching_core")

A common `OrderMatchingCore` for the `OrderMatchingEngine` and other components.

[matching\_engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/matching_engine/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::matching_engine")

Order matching engine components for simulating trading venue behavior.

[models](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/models/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::models")

Execution models and data structures.

[order\_emulator](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/order_emulator/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::order_emulator")

Order emulation components for simulating order execution behavior.

[order\_manager](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/order_manager/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::order_manager")

Order management component.

[protection](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/protection/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::protection")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/python/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[reconciliation](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/reconciliation/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::reconciliation")

Execution state reconciliation functions.

[trailing](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/trailing/index.html "mod nautilus_execution::trailing")

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

# nautilus_indicators - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_indicators](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_indicators Copy item path
=========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_indicators/lib.rs.html#16-74)

Expand description

Technical analysis indicators for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-indicators` crate provides a comprehensive collection of technical analysis indicators for quantitative trading and market research. This includes a wide variety of indicators organized by category, with a unified trait-based architecture for consistent usage:

*   **Moving averages**: SMA, EMA, DEMA, HMA, WMA, VWAP, adaptive averages, and linear regression.
*   **Momentum indicators**: RSI, MACD, Aroon, Bollinger Bands, CCI, Stochastics, and rate of change.
*   **Volatility indicators**: ATR, Donchian Channels, Keltner Channels, and volatility ratios.
*   **Ratio analysis**: Efficiency ratios and spread analysis for relative performance.
*   **Order book indicators**: Book imbalance ratio for analyzing market microstructure.
*   **Common indicator trait**: Unified interface supporting bars, quotes, trades, and order book data.

All indicators are designed for high-performance real-time processing with bounded memory usage and efficient circular buffer implementations. The crate supports both Rust-native usage and Python integration for strategy development and backtesting.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_indicators/index.html#feature-flags)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

# nautilus_infrastructure - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_infrastructure](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_infrastructure Copy item path
=============================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_infrastructure/lib.rs.html#16-69)

Expand description

Database and messaging infrastructure for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-infrastructure` crate provides backend database implementations and message bus adapters that enable NautilusTrader to scale from development to production deployments. This includes enterprise-grade data persistence and messaging capabilities:

*   **Redis integration**: Cache database and message bus implementations using Redis.
*   **PostgreSQL integration**: SQL-based cache database with comprehensive data models.
*   **Connection management**: Robust connection handling with retry logic and health monitoring.
*   **Serialization options**: Support for JSON and MessagePack encoding formats.
*   **Python bindings**: PyO3 integration for seamless Python interoperability.

The crate supports multiple database backends through feature flags, allowing users to choose the appropriate infrastructure components for their specific deployment requirements and scale.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#platform)
Platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `redis`: Enables the Redis cache database and message bus backing implementations.
*   `postgres`: Enables the PostgreSQL SQLx models and cache database backend.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/index.html#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/python/index.html "mod nautilus_infrastructure::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[redis](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/redis/index.html "mod nautilus_infrastructure::redis")

Provides a Redis backed `CacheDatabase` and `MessageBusDatabase` implementation.

[sql](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_infrastructure/sql/index.html "mod nautilus_infrastructure::sql")

SQL database implementations and utilities.

Expand description

Kraken exchange adapter for NautilusTrader.

This adapter provides integration with the Kraken cryptocurrency exchange, supporting both Spot and Futures markets.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#features)
Features
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   REST API v2 client for market data and account operations.
*   WebSocket v2 client for real-time data feeds.
*   Support for Spot and Futures markets.
*   Comprehensive instrument, ticker, trade, orderbook, and OHLC data.
*   Prepared for execution support (orders, positions, balances).

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_kraken/index.html#api-documentation)
API Documentation
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Kraken REST API](https://docs.kraken.com/api/)
    
*   [Kraken WebSocket v2](https://docs.kraken.com/websockets-v2/)

 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

# nautilus_live - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_live Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_live/lib.rs.html#16-71)

Expand description

Live system node for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-live` crate provides high-level abstractions and infrastructure for running live trading systems, including data streaming, execution management, and system lifecycle handling. It builds on top of the system kernel to provide simplified interfaces for live deployment:

*   `LiveNode` High-level abstraction for live system nodes.
*   `LiveNodeConfig` Configuration for live node deployment.
*   `AsyncRunner` for managing system real-time data flow.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `streaming`: Enables `persistence` dependency for streaming configuration.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
     (auto-enables `streaming`).
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#reexports)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use emitter::[ExecutionEventEmitter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/struct.ExecutionEventEmitter.html "struct nautilus_live::emitter::ExecutionEventEmitter") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[builder](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/builder/index.html "mod nautilus_live::builder")

Builder for constructing [`LiveNode`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/node/struct.LiveNode.html "struct nautilus_live::node::LiveNode")
 instances.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/config/index.html "mod nautilus_live::config")

Configuration types for live Nautilus system nodes.

[emitter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/index.html "mod nautilus_live::emitter")

Live execution event emitter for async event dispatch.

[manager](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/manager/index.html "mod nautilus_live::manager")

Execution state manager for live trading.

[node](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/node/index.html "mod nautilus_live::node")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/python/index.html "mod nautilus_live::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[runner](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/runner/index.html "mod nautilus_live::runner")

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html#structs)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Base implementation for execution clients providing identity and connection state.

Factory for generating order and account events.

Crate nautilus\_model Copy item path
====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_model/lib.rs.html#16-80)

Expand description

Trading domain model for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-model` crate provides a type-safe domain model that forms the backbone of the framework and can serve as the foundation for building algorithmic trading systems.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `stubs`: Enables type stubs for use in testing scenarios.
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `defi`: Enables the DeFi (Decentralized Finance) domain model.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enumerations for the trading domain model.

Events for the trading domain model.

C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
.

Identifiers for the trading domain model.

[instruments](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/instruments/index.html "mod nautilus_model::instruments")

Instrument definitions for the trading domain model.

Model specific macros.

.

# nautilus_network - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_network](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_network Copy item path
======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_network/lib.rs.html#16-87)

Expand description

Network functionality for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-network` crate provides networking components including HTTP, WebSocket, and raw TCP socket clients, rate limiting, backoff strategies, and socket TLS utilities for connecting to trading venues and data providers.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.
*   `turmoil`: Enables deterministic network simulation testing with [turmoil](https://github.com/tokio-rs/turmoil)
    .

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#testing)
Testing
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The crate includes both standard integration tests and deterministic network simulation tests using turmoil.

To run standard tests:

    cargo nextest run -p nautilus-network

To run turmoil network simulation tests:

    cargo nextest run -p nautilus-network --features turmoil

The turmoil tests simulate various network conditions (reconnections, partitions, etc.) in a deterministic way, allowing reliable testing of network failure scenarios without flakiness.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[backoff](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/backoff/index.html "mod nautilus_network::backoff")

Provides an implementation of an exponential backoff mechanism with jitter support. It is used for managing reconnection delays in the socket clients.

[error](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/error/index.html "mod nautilus_network::error")

Network error types.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/http/index.html "mod nautilus_network::http")

A high-performance HTTP client implementation.

[mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/mode/index.html "mod nautilus_network::mode")

Connection mode enumeration for socket clients.

[net](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/index.html "mod nautilus_network::net")

Network abstractions for dependency injection and testing.

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/python/index.html "mod nautilus_network::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

[ratelimiter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/ratelimiter/index.html "mod nautilus_network::ratelimiter")

A rate limiter implementation heavily inspired by [governor](https://github.com/antifuchs/governor)
.

[retry](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/retry/index.html "mod nautilus_network::retry")

Generic retry mechanism for network operations.

[socket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/socket/index.html "mod nautilus_network::socket")

High-performance raw TCP client implementation with TLS capability, automatic reconnection with exponential backoff and state management.

[websocket](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/websocket/index.html "mod nautilus_network::websocket")

WebSocket client implementation with automatic reconnection and subscription tracking.

Constants[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html#constants)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[RECONNECTED](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/constant.RECONNECTED.html "constant nautilus_network::RECONNECTED")

Sentinel message to signal reconnection completion to Rust consumers.

---

# nautilus_okx - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_okx](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_okx Copy item path
==================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_okx/lib.rs.html#16-75)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [OKX](https://www.okx.com/)
 cryptocurrency exchange.

The `nautilus-okx` crate provides client bindings (HTTP & WebSocket), data models and helper utilities that wrap the official **OKX v5 API**.

The official OKX API reference can be found at [https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/](https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/en/)
. All public links inside this crate reference the English version.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#platform)
Platform
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#reexports)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::common::enums::[OKXInstrumentType](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/enums/enum.OKXInstrumentType.html "enum nautilus_okx::common::enums::OKXInstrumentType") ;`

`pub use crate::common::enums::[OKXOrderType](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/enums/enum.OKXOrderType.html "enum nautilus_okx::common::enums::OKXOrderType") ;`

`pub use crate::common::enums::[OKXPositionMode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/enums/enum.OKXPositionMode.html "enum nautilus_okx::common::enums::OKXPositionMode") ;`

`pub use crate::common::enums::[OKXPositionSide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/enums/enum.OKXPositionSide.html "enum nautilus_okx::common::enums::OKXPositionSide") ;`

`pub use crate::common::enums::[OKXSide](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/enums/enum.OKXSide.html "enum nautilus_okx::common::enums::OKXSide") ;`

`pub use crate::common::models::[OKXInstrument](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/models/struct.OKXInstrument.html "struct nautilus_okx::common::models::OKXInstrument") ;`

`pub use crate::data::[OKXDataClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/data/struct.OKXDataClient.html "struct nautilus_okx::data::OKXDataClient") ;`

`pub use crate::execution::[OKXExecutionClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/execution/struct.OKXExecutionClient.html "struct nautilus_okx::execution::OKXExecutionClient") ;`

`pub use crate::http::client::[OKXHttpClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/http/client/struct.OKXHttpClient.html "struct nautilus_okx::http::client::OKXHttpClient") ;`

`pub use crate::http::error::[OKXHttpError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/http/error/enum.OKXHttpError.html "enum nautilus_okx::http::error::OKXHttpError") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::client::[OKXWebSocketClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/websocket/client/struct.OKXWebSocketClient.html "struct nautilus_okx::websocket::client::OKXWebSocketClient") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::error::[OKXWsError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/websocket/error/enum.OKXWsError.html "enum nautilus_okx::websocket::error::OKXWsError") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common")

Common functionality shared across the OKX adapter.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/config/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::config")

Configuration structures for the OKX adapter.

# nautilus_persistence - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_persistence](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_persistence Copy item path
==========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_persistence/lib.rs.html#16-55)

Expand description

Data persistence and storage management for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-persistence` crate provides data persistence capabilities for storing and retrieving trading data, state, and configuration.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#platform)
Platform
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `cloud`: Enables cloud storage backends (S3, Azure, GCP, HTTP) via `object_store`.
*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) from [cbindgen](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)
    .
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
     (auto-enables `cloud`).
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/index.html#modules)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[backend](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/backend/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::backend")

Provides an Apache Parquet backend powered by [DataFusion](https://arrow.apache.org/datafusion)
.

[config](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/config/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::config")

[parquet](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/parquet/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::parquet")

[python](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_persistence/python/index.html "mod nautilus_persistence::python")

Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
.

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for the [Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/)
 prediction market.

The `nautilus-polymarket` crate provides integration with the Polymarket CLOB API for trading binary outcome contracts on the Polymarket prediction market.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_polymarket/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_polymarket/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

# nautilus_pyo3 - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_pyo3](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_pyo3 Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_pyo3/lib.rs.html#16-354)

Expand description

Python bindings aggregator crate for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-pyo3` crate collects the Python bindings generated across the NautilusTrader workspace and re-exports them through a single shared library that can be included in binary wheels.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#platform)
Platform
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate is primarily intended to be built for Python via [maturin](https://github.com/PyO3/maturin)
 and therefore provides a broad set of feature flags to toggle bindings and optional dependencies:

*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module (automatically enabled by `maturin`).
*   `ffi`: Enables the C foreign function interface (FFI) support in dependent crates.
*   `high-precision`: Uses 128-bit value types throughout the workspace.
*   `cython-compat`: Adjusts the module name so it can be imported from Cython generated code.
*   `postgres`: Enables PostgreSQL (sqlx) back-ends in dependent crates.
*   `redis`: Enables Redis based infrastructure in dependent crates.
*   `hypersync`: Enables hypersync support (fast parallel hash maps) where available.
*   `tracing-bridge`: Enables the `tracing` subscriber bridge for log integration.
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support including blockchain adapters.

Functions[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_pyo3/index.html#functions)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[stub\_info](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_pyo3/fn.stub_info.html "fn nautilus_pyo3::stub_info")

Generate Python type stub info for PyO3 bindings.

---

# nautilus_portfolio - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_portfolio](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_portfolio Copy item path
========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_portfolio/lib.rs.html#16-66)

Expand description

Portfolio management and risk analysis for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-portfolio` crate provides comprehensive portfolio management capabilities including real-time position tracking, performance calculations, and risk management. This includes sophisticated portfolio analytics and multi-currency support:

*   **Portfolio tracking**: Real-time portfolio state management with position and balance monitoring.
*   **Account management**: Support for cash and margin accounts across multiple venues.
*   **Performance calculations**: Real-time unrealized PnL, realized PnL, and mark-to-market valuations.
*   **Risk management**: Initial margin calculations, maintenance margin tracking, and exposure monitoring.
*   **Multi-currency support**: Currency conversion and cross-currency risk exposure analysis.
*   **Configuration options**: Flexible settings for price types, currency conversion, and portfolio behavior.

The crate handles complex portfolio scenarios including multi-venue trading, currency conversions, and sophisticated margin calculations for both live trading and backtesting environments.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#platform)
Platform
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

# nautilus_risk - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_risk/index.html#main-content)

Crate nautilus\_risk Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_risk/lib.rs.html#16-61)

Expand description

Risk engine for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-risk` crate provides comprehensive risk management capabilities including pre-trade order validation, position sizing calculations, and trading controls. This system ensures trading operations remain within defined risk parameters and regulatory constraints:

*   **Risk engine**: Central risk management orchestration with configurable trading states.
*   **Order validation**: Pre-trade checks for price, quantity, notional limits, and market conditions.
*   **Position sizing**: Fixed-risk position sizing calculations with commission and exchange rate support.
*   **Trading controls**: Rate limiting, balance validation, and exposure management.
*   **Account protection**: Multi-currency balance checks and margin requirement validation.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_risk/index.html#platform)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .

Crate nautilus\_sandbox Copy item path
======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_sandbox/lib.rs.html#16-61)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 sandbox execution adapter.

The `nautilus-sandbox` crate provides a simulated execution client that uses the `OrderMatchingEngine` to simulate order execution against live market data. This enables paper trading and strategy testing in real-time without actual order execution on exchanges.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_sandbox/index.html#platform)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

# nautilus_system - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_system](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_system Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/lib.rs.html#16-74)

Expand description

System-level components and orchestration for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-system` crate provides the core system architecture for orchestrating trading systems, including the kernel that manages all engines, configuration management, and system-level factories for creating components:

*   `NautilusKernel` - Core system orchestrator managing engines and components.
*   `NautilusKernelConfig` - Configuration for kernel initialization.
*   System builders and factories for component creation.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `streaming`: Enables `persistence` dependency for streaming configuration.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
     (auto-enables `streaming`).
*   `defi`: Enables DeFi (Decentralized Finance) support.
*   `live`: Enables live trading mode dependencies.
*   `tracing-bridge`: Enables the `tracing` subscriber bridge for log integration.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html#reexports)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use builder::[NautilusKernelBuilder](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/builder/struct.NautilusKernelBuilder.html "struct nautilus_system::builder::NautilusKernelBuilder") ;`

`pub use config::[NautilusKernelConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/trait.NautilusKernelConfig.html "trait nautilus_system::config::NautilusKernelConfig") ;`

`pub use config::[RotationConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/enum.RotationConfig.html "enum nautilus_system::config::RotationConfig") ;`

`pub use config::[StreamingConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/struct.StreamingConfig.html "struct nautilus_system::config::StreamingConfig") ;`

`pub use factories::[ClientConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.ClientConfig.html "trait nautilus_system::factories::ClientConfig") ;`

`pub use factories::[DataClientFactory](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.DataClientFactory.html "trait nautilus_system::factories::DataClientFactory") ;`

`pub use factories::[ExecutionClientFactory](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.ExecutionClientFactory.html "trait nautilus_system::factories::ExecutionClientFactory") ;`

# nautilus_serialization - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_serialization](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_serialization Copy item path
============================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_serialization/lib.rs.html#16-132)

Expand description

Data serialization and format conversion for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-serialization` crate provides comprehensive data serialization capabilities for converting trading data between different formats including Apache Arrow and Cap’n Proto. This enables efficient data storage, retrieval, and interoperability across different systems:

*   **Apache Arrow integration**: Schema definitions and encoding/decoding for market data types.
*   **Parquet file operations**: High-performance columnar storage for historical data analysis.
*   **Record batch processing**: Efficient batch operations for time-series data.
*   **Schema management**: Type-safe schema definitions with metadata preservation.
*   **Cross-format conversion**: Seamless data interchange between Arrow, Cap’n Proto, and native types.
*   **Cap’n Proto serialization**: Zero-copy, schema-based serialization for efficient data interchange (requires `capnp` feature).
*   **SBE decode utilities**: Zero-copy cursor and shared decode errors for SBE parsers (requires `sbe` feature).

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `arrow`: Enables Apache Arrow schema definitions and RecordBatch encoding/decoding.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.
*   `capnp`: Enables [Cap’n Proto](https://capnproto.org/)
     serialization support.
*   `sbe`: Enables generic SBE (Simple Binary Encoding) decode utilities.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/index.html#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# nautilus_trader - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_trader/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_trader](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_trader/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_trader Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_trader/lib.rs.html#16-27)

Expand description

Workspace shim crate for _NautilusTrader_.

This crate does **not** expose any public API of its own – it exists solely so that the root `crates/` directory is a valid Cargo crate. Having a real compilation unit prevents Cargo from discarding incremental build artefacts for the workspace when only the dependency graph changes, which in turn keeps rebuild times predictable.

Because downstream users should never depend on this shim directly, the crate is marked `publish = false` in its `Cargo.toml`.

If you are looking for the public entry-points of NautilusTrader, refer to the individual crates under `crates/*` such as `nautilus-core`, `nautilus-model`, `nautilus-backtest`, etc.

---

# nautilus_testkit - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_testkit/index.html#main-content)

[Crate nautilus\_testkit](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_testkit/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crate nautilus\_testkit Copy item path
======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_testkit/lib.rs.html#16-67)

Expand description

Test utilities and data management for [NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
.

The `nautilus-testkit` crate provides comprehensive testing utilities including test data management, file handling, and common testing patterns. This crate supports robust testing workflows across the entire NautilusTrader ecosystem with automated data downloads and validation:

*   **Test data management**: Automated downloading and caching of test datasets.
*   **File utilities**: File integrity verification with SHA-256 checksums.
*   **Path resolution**: Platform-agnostic test data path management.
*   **Precision handling**: Support for both 64-bit and 128-bit precision test data.
*   **Common patterns**: Reusable test utilities and helper functions.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_testkit/index.html#platform)
Platform
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_testkit/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `high-precision`: Enables [high-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
     to use 128-bit value types.
*   `extension-module`: Builds the crate as a Python extension module.

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_tardis/index.html#main-content)

=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_tardis/lib.rs.html#16-66)

Expand description

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 adapter for [Tardis](https://tardis.dev/)
.

The `nautilus-tardis` crate provides integration with the Tardis API for accessing normalized historical and real-time market data across multiple exchanges.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_tardis/index.html#platform)
Platform
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[NautilusTrader](http://nautilustrader.io/)
 is an open-source, high-performance, production-grade algorithmic trading platform, providing quantitative traders with the ability to backtest portfolios of automated trading strategies on historical data with an event-driven engine, and also deploy those same strategies live, with no code changes.

NautilusTrader’s design, architecture, and implementation philosophy prioritizes software correctness and safety at the highest level, with the aim of supporting mission-critical, trading system backtesting and live deployment workloads.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_tardis/index.html#feature-flags)
Feature Flags
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides feature flags to control source code inclusion during compilation, depending on the intended use case, i.e. whether to provide Python bindings for the [nautilus\_trader](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus_trader)
 Python package, or as part of a Rust only build.

*   `replay` (default): Enables market data replay functionality.
*   `python`: Enables Python bindings from [PyO3](https://pyo3.rs/)
    .
*   `extension-module`: Builds as a Python extension module (used with `python`).

[High-precision mode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/nightly/getting_started/installation#precision-mode)
 (128-bit value types) is enabled by default.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_tardis/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html)

Macro log\_error Copy item path
===============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_common/logging/macros.rs.html#306-361)

    macro_rules! log_error {
        ($msg:literal, color = $color:expr, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, color = $color:expr, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, $arg2:expr, color = $color:expr, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($msg:literal, component = $component:expr, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, component = $component:expr, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, $arg2:expr, component = $component:expr, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($msg:literal, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, $arg2:expr, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($msg:literal, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, $arg2:expr, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $arg1:expr, $arg2:expr, $arg3:expr, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($msg:literal) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $($args:expr),+) => { ... };
    }

Expand description

Logs an error message with automatic red color or custom color and component.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_error.html#usage)
Usage
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // Automatic color (red)
    log_error!("Failed to connect to exchange: {}", error);
    
    // Custom color
    log_error!("Custom error message", color = LogColor::Magenta);
    
    // Custom component
    log_error!("Connection failed", component = "DataEngine");
    
    // Both color and component (flexible order)
    log_error!("Critical error", color = LogColor::Magenta, component = "DataEngine");
    log_error!("Critical error", component = "DataEngine", color = LogColor::Magenta);

---

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/math.rs.html#49-55)

    macro_rules! approx_eq {
        ($type:ty, $left:expr, $right:expr, epsilon = $epsilon:expr) => { ... };
    }

Expand description

Macro for approximate floating-point equality comparison.

This macro compares two floating-point values with a specified epsilon tolerance, providing a safe alternative to exact equality checks which can fail due to floating-point precision issues.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/macro.approx_eq.html#usage)
Usage
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    use nautilus_core::approx_eq;
    
    let a = 0.1 + 0.2;
    let b = 0.3;
    assert!(approx_eq!(f64, a, b, epsilon = 1e-10));

---

# MUTEX_POISONED in nautilus_core - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/constant.MUTEX_POISONED.html#main-content)

[MUTEX\_POISONED](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/constant.MUTEX_POISONED.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html)

Constant MUTEX\_POISONED Copy item path
=======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/lib.rs.html#109)

    pub const MUTEX_POISONED: &str = "Mutex poisoned";

Expand description

Message for when a mutex guard cannot be acquired due to poisoning.

Mutex guards should use `expect` rather than handle poison errors. A poisoned mutex indicates a thread panicked while holding the lock, meaning protected data may be in an inconsistent state. Propagating the panic is the idiomatic and safe approach, as continuing with potentially corrupted data would violate safety invariants.

---

    macro_rules! log_trace {
        ($msg:literal, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $($args:expr),+, component = $component:expr) => { ... };
        ($msg:literal, color = $color:expr) => { ... };
        ($fmt:literal, $($args:expr),+, color = $color:expr) => { ... };

    }

Expand description

Logs a trace message with automatic color mapping or custom color and component.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/macro.log_trace.html#usage)
Usage
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    // Automatic color (normal)
    log_trace!("Processing tick data");
    
    // Custom color
    log_trace!("Processing tick data", color = LogColor::Cyan);
    
    // Custom component
    log_trace!("Processing data", component = "DataEngine");
    
    // Both color and component (flexible order)
    log_trace!("Data processed", color = LogColor::Cyan, component = "DataEngine");
    log_trace!("Data processed", component = "DataEngine", color = LogColor::Cyan);

# nautilus_core::drop - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/drop/index.html#main-content)

[Module drop](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/drop/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html)

Module drop Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/drop.rs.html#16-33)

Expand description

Explicit, manually-invocable cleanup hook used to break reference cycles before `Drop`.

Many long-lived components register callbacks or handlers that retain strong references back to them, creating reference-count cycles that prevent Rust’s automatic destructor (`Drop`) from running. The `CleanDrop` trait provides an _object-safe_ method, `clean_drop`, that can be called explicitly (e.g. during an orderly shutdown) to release such resources. Implementations should also call `clean_drop` from their `Drop` impl as a final safety net.

Design contract:

1.  **Idempotent** – multiple calls must be safe.
2.  Perform all externally-observable cleanup here (unregister handlers, abort tasks, clear callbacks, downgrade `Rc`/`Arc` references, etc.).

Traits[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/drop/index.html#traits)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[CleanDrop](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/drop/trait.CleanDrop.html "trait nautilus_core::drop::CleanDrop")

Trait providing an explicit cleanup method that may be invoked prior to `Drop`.

---
