# BacktestNode in nautilus_backtest::node - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#main-content)

[BacktestNode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_backtest](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/index.html)
::[node](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/index.html)

Struct BacktestNode Copy item path
==================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#49-52)

    pub struct BacktestNode { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

Orchestrates catalog-driven backtests from run configurations.

`BacktestNode` connects the \[`ParquetDataCatalog`\] with [`BacktestEngine`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/struct.BacktestEngine.html "struct nautilus_backtest::engine::BacktestEngine")
 to load historical data and run backtests. Supports both oneshot and streaming modes.

Implementations[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#54-274)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#impl-BacktestNode)

### impl [BacktestNode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html "struct nautilus_backtest::node::BacktestNode")

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#65-72)

#### pub fn [new](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.new)
(configs: [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[BacktestRunConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/config/struct.BacktestRunConfig.html "struct nautilus_backtest::config::BacktestRunConfig")
\>) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/anyhow/1.0.102/anyhow/type.Result.html "type anyhow::Result")
<Self>

Creates a new [`BacktestNode`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html "struct nautilus_backtest::node::BacktestNode")
 instance.

Validates that configs are non-empty and internally consistent:

*   All data config instrument venues must have a matching venue config.
*   L2/L3 book types require order book data in the data configs.
*   Data config time ranges must be valid (start <= end).

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#errors)
Errors

Returns an error if `configs` is empty or validation fails.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#76-78)

#### pub fn [configs](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.configs)
(&self) -> &\[[BacktestRunConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/config/struct.BacktestRunConfig.html "struct nautilus_backtest::config::BacktestRunConfig")\
\]

Returns the run configurations.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#88-180)

#### pub fn [build](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.build)
(&mut self) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/anyhow/1.0.102/anyhow/type.Result.html "type anyhow::Result")
<[()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html)
\>

Builds backtest engines from the run configurations.

For each config, creates a [`BacktestEngine`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/struct.BacktestEngine.html "struct nautilus_backtest::engine::BacktestEngine")
, adds venues, and loads instruments from the catalog.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#errors-1)
Errors

Returns an error if engine creation, venue setup, or instrument loading fails.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#184-186)

#### pub fn [get\_engine\_mut](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.get_engine_mut)
(&mut self, id: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<&mut [BacktestEngine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/struct.BacktestEngine.html "struct nautilus_backtest::engine::BacktestEngine")
\>

Returns a mutable reference to the engine for the given run config ID.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#190-192)

#### pub fn [get\_engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.get_engine)
(&self, id: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<&[BacktestEngine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/struct.BacktestEngine.html "struct nautilus_backtest::engine::BacktestEngine")
\>

Returns a reference to the engine for the given run config ID.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#196-198)

#### pub fn [get\_engines](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.get_engines)
(&self) -> [Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<&[BacktestEngine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/struct.BacktestEngine.html "struct nautilus_backtest::engine::BacktestEngine")
\>

Returns all created backtest engines.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#209-243)

#### pub fn [run](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.run)
(&mut self) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/anyhow/1.0.102/anyhow/type.Result.html "type anyhow::Result")
<[Vec](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct alloc::vec::Vec")
<[BacktestResult](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/engine/struct.BacktestResult.html "struct nautilus_backtest::engine::BacktestResult")
\>>

Runs all configured backtests and returns results.

Automatically calls [`build()`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.build "method nautilus_backtest::node::BacktestNode::build")
 if engines have not been created yet. For each run config, loads data from the catalog and runs the engine. Supports both oneshot (`chunk_size = None`) and streaming modes.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#errors-2)
Errors

Returns an error if building, data loading, or engine execution fails.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_backtest/node.rs.html#250-252)

) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/anyhow/1.0.102/anyhow/type.Result.html "type anyhow::Result")
<ParquetDataCatalog>

Creates a \[`ParquetDataCatalog`\] from a data config.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

where T: IntoPyObject<'py>,

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_backtest/node/struct.BacktestNode.html#method.into_bound_py_any)

#### fn into\_bound\_py\_any(self, py: Python<'py>) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecutionClientFactory.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

Mutably borrows `self`, then passes `self.as_mut()` into the pipe function.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecutionClientFactory.html#method.pipe_deref)

#### fn pipe\_deref<'a, T, R>(&'a self, func: impl [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")

) -> R) -> R

where Self: [Deref](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/deref/trait.Deref.html "trait core::ops::deref::Deref")
<Target = T>, T: 'a + ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
, R: 'a,

Borrows `self`, then passes `self.deref()` into the pipe function.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecutionClientFactory.html#method.pipe_deref_mut)

#### fn pipe\_deref\_mut<'a, T, R>( &'a mut self, func: impl [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
([&'a mut T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
) -> R, ) -> R

where Self: [DerefMut](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/deref/trait.DerefMut.html "trait core::ops::deref::DerefMut")
<Target = T> + [Deref](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/deref/trait.Deref.html "trait core::ops::deref::Deref")
, T: 'a + ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
, R: 'a,

Mutably borrows `self`, then passes `self.deref_mut()` into the pipe function.

 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/execution/struct.HyperliquidExecutionClient.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/execution/struct.HyperliquidExecutionClient.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

Provides order management, account operations, and position management for Kraken Futures markets.

# HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig in nautilus_hyperliquid::factories - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig.html#main-content)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_hyperliquid](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/index.html)
::[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/index.html)

Struct HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig Copy item path
==================================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories.rs.html#120-127)

    pub struct HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig {
        pub trader_id: TraderId,
        pub account_id: AccountId,
        pub config: HyperliquidExecClientConfig,
    }

Expand description

Configuration for creating Hyperliquid execution clients via factory.

This wraps [`HyperliquidExecClientConfig`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidExecClientConfig.html "struct nautilus_hyperliquid::config::HyperliquidExecClientConfig")
 with the additional trader and account identifiers required by the \[`ExecutionClientCore`\].

Fields[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig.html#fields)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig.html#structfield.trader_id)
`trader_id: TraderId`

The trader ID for the execution client.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig.html#structfield.account_id)
`account_id: AccountId`

The account ID for the execution client.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig.html#structfield.config)

The underlying execution client configuration.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/factories/struct.HyperliquidExecFactoryConfig.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

 = T::NAME

👎Deprecated since 0.27.0:

Use ::classinfo\_object() instead and format the type name at runtime. Note that using built-in cast features is often better than manual PyTypeCheck usage.

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/data/struct.HyperliquidDataClient.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/data/struct.HyperliquidDataClient.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

# nautilus_data::defi - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/index.html#main-content)

[Module defi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html)

Module defi Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_data/defi/mod.rs.html#16-29)

Expand description

DeFi (Decentralized Finance) integration for the data crate.

This module provides centralized access to DeFi functionality throughout the data crate. DeFi support includes client subscriptions and engine processing.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/index.html#feature-flag)
Feature Flag
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All DeFi functionality requires the `defi` feature flag to be enabled:

    nautilus-data = { version = "0.x", features = ["defi"] }

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[client](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/client/index.html "mod nautilus_data::defi::client")

DeFi-specific data client functionality.

[engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/defi/engine/index.html "mod nautilus_data::defi::engine")

DeFi-specific data engine functionality.

---

# nautilus_core::nanos - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#main-content)

[Module nanos](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html)

Module nanos Copy item path
===========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/nanos.rs.html#16-1309)

Expand description

A `UnixNanos` type for working with timestamps in nanoseconds since the UNIX epoch.

This module provides a strongly-typed representation of timestamps as nanoseconds since the UNIX epoch (January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC). The `UnixNanos` type offers conversion utilities, arithmetic operations, and comparison methods.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#features)
Features
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Zero-cost abstraction with appropriate operator implementations.
*   Conversion to/from `DateTime<Utc>`.
*   RFC 3339 string formatting.
*   Duration calculations.
*   Flexible parsing and serialization.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#parsing-and-serialization)
Parsing and Serialization
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`UnixNanos` can be created from and serialized to various formats:

*   Integer values are interpreted as nanoseconds since the UNIX epoch.
*   Floating-point values are interpreted as seconds since the UNIX epoch (converted to nanoseconds using truncation, not rounding, for consistency with [`secs_to_nanos`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/datetime/fn.secs_to_nanos.html "fn nautilus_core::datetime::secs_to_nanos")
    ).
*   String values may be:
    *   A numeric string (interpreted as nanoseconds).
    *   A floating-point string (interpreted as seconds, converted to nanoseconds).
    *   An RFC 3339 formatted timestamp (ISO 8601 with timezone).
    *   A simple date string in YYYY-MM-DD format (interpreted as midnight UTC on that date).

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#limitations)
Limitations
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Negative timestamps are invalid and will result in an error.
*   Arithmetic operations will panic on overflow/underflow rather than wrapping.
*   The `as_i64()` method and `DateTime<Utc>` conversions will panic for timestamps beyond approximately year 2262 (when nanoseconds exceed `i64::MAX`).

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#structs)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[UnixNanos](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/struct.UnixNanos.html "struct nautilus_core::nanos::UnixNanos")

Represents a timestamp in nanoseconds since the UNIX epoch.

Type Aliases[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/index.html#types)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[DurationNanos](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/nanos/type.DurationNanos.html "type nautilus_core::nanos::DurationNanos")

Represents a duration in nanoseconds.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidExecClientConfig.html#structfield.private_key)
`private_key: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option") <[String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String") >`

Private key for signing transactions.

If not provided, falls back to environment variable:

*   Mainnet: `HYPERLIQUID_PK`
*   Testnet: `HYPERLIQUID_TESTNET_PK`

Note: WebSocket proxy support is not yet implemented. This field is reserved for future functionality. Use `http_proxy_url` for REST API proxy support.

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidExecClientConfig.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidExecClientConfig.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

Struct DydxDataClient Copy item path

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_dydx/data.rs.html#100-151)

    pub struct DydxDataClient { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

dYdX data client for live market data streaming and historical data requests.

This client integrates with the Nautilus DataEngine to provide:

*   Real-time market data via WebSocket subscriptions
*   Historical data via REST API requests
*   Automatic instrument discovery and caching
*   Connection lifecycle management

 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_dydx/data/struct.DydxDataClient.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_dydx/data/struct.DydxDataClient.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

# ExecutionEventEmitter in nautilus_live::emitter - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/struct.ExecutionEventEmitter.html#main-content)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html)
::[emitter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/index.html)

Struct ExecutionEventEmitter Copy item path
===========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_live/emitter.rs.html#59-63)

    pub struct ExecutionEventEmitter { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

Event emitter for live trading - combines event generation with async dispatch.

This struct wraps an [`OrderEventFactory`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/struct.OrderEventFactory.html "struct nautilus_live::OrderEventFactory")
 for event construction and an unbounded channel sender for async dispatch. It provides `emit_*` convenience methods that generate and send events in a single call.

The sender is set during the adapter’s `start()` phase via [`set_sender`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/struct.ExecutionEventEmitter.html#method.set_sender "method nautilus_live::emitter::ExecutionEventEmitter::set_sender")
.

Implementations[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/struct.ExecutionEventEmitter.html#implementations)

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/struct.ExecutionEventEmitter.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/emitter/struct.ExecutionEventEmitter.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

Kraken Spot execution client.

Provides order management and account operations for Kraken Spot markets.

# OrderEventFactory in nautilus_live - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/struct.OrderEventFactory.html#main-content)

[OrderEventFactory](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/struct.OrderEventFactory.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html)

Struct OrderEventFactory Copy item path
=======================================

    pub struct OrderEventFactory { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

Factory for generating order and account events.

This struct holds the identity information needed to construct events and provides methods to generate all order event types. It is `Clone` and `Send`, allowing it to be used in async contexts.

Implementations[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/struct.OrderEventFactory.html#implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")

    }

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidDataClientConfig.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_hyperliquid/config/struct.HyperliquidDataClientConfig.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

# ExecutionClientCore in nautilus_live - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/struct.ExecutionClientCore.html#main-content)

[ExecutionClientCore](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/struct.ExecutionClientCore.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_live](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_live/index.html)

Struct ExecutionClientCore Copy item path
=========================================

    pub struct ExecutionClientCore {
        pub trader_id: TraderId,
        pub client_id: ClientId,
        pub venue: Venue,
        pub oms_type: OmsType,
        pub account_id: AccountId,
        pub account_type: AccountType,
        pub base_currency: Option<Currency>,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

Base implementation for execution clients providing identity and connection state.

This struct provides the foundation for all execution clients, holding client identity, connection state, and read-only cache access. Execution clients use this as a base and extend it with venue-specific implementations.

For event generation, use `OrderEventFactory` from `nautilus_common::factories`. For live adapters, use `ExecutionEventEmitter` which combines event generation with async dispatch. For backtest/sandbox, use `OrderEventFactory` directly and dispatch via `msgbus::send_order_event()`.

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

Kraken Futures data client.

Provides real-time market data from Kraken Futures markets.

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

    pub struct KrakenDataClientConfig {
        pub api_key: Option<String>,
        pub api_secret: Option<String>,
        pub product_type: KrakenProductType,
        pub environment: KrakenEnvironment,
        pub base_url: Option<String>,
        pub ws_public_url: Option<String>,
        pub ws_private_url: Option<String>,
        pub http_proxy: Option<String>,
        pub ws_proxy: Option<String>,
        pub timeout_secs: Option<u64>,
        pub heartbeat_interval_secs: Option<u64>,
        pub max_requests_per_second: Option<u32>,
    }

Expand description

Configuration for the Kraken data client.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_kraken/config/struct.KrakenDataClientConfig.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

# StackStr in nautilus_core::stack_str - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#main-content)

[StackStr](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html)
::[stack\_str](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/index.html)

Struct StackStr Copy item path
==============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/stack_str.rs.html#79-84)

    pub struct StackStr { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

A stack-allocated ASCII string with a maximum capacity of 36 characters.

Optimized for short identifier strings with:

*   Stack allocation (no heap).
*   `Copy` semantics.
*   O(1) length access.
*   C FFI compatibility (null-terminated).

ASCII is required to guarantee 1 character == 1 byte, ensuring the buffer always holds exactly the capacity in characters. This aligns with identifier conventions which are inherently ASCII.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#memory-layout)
Memory Layout
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `value` field is placed first so the struct pointer equals the string pointer, making C FFI more natural: `(char*)&stack_str` works directly.

Implementations[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#implementations)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Panics

Panics if:

*   `s` is empty or contains only whitespace.
*   `s` contains non-ASCII characters or interior NUL bytes.
*   `s` exceeds 36 characters.

#### pub unsafe fn [from\_c\_ptr](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.from_c_ptr)
(ptr: [\*const](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.pointer.html)
 [c\_char](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ffi/primitives/type.c_char.html "type core::ffi::primitives::c_char")
) -> Self

Creates a [`StackStr`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html "struct nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr")
 from a C string pointer.

For untrusted input from C code, use [`from_c_ptr_checked`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.from_c_ptr_checked "associated function nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr::from_c_ptr_checked")
 to avoid panics crossing FFI boundaries.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#safety)
Safety

*   `ptr` must be a valid pointer to a null-terminated C string.
*   The string must contain only valid ASCII (no interior NUL bytes).
*   The string must not exceed 36 characters.

Violating these requirements causes a panic. If this function is called from C code, such a panic is undefined behavior.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/stack_str.rs.html#198-203)

#### pub unsafe fn [from\_c\_ptr\_checked](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.from_c_ptr_checked)
(ptr: [\*const](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.pointer.html)
 [c\_char](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ffi/primitives/type.c_char.html "type core::ffi::primitives::c_char")
) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<Self>

Creates a [`StackStr`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html "struct nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr")
 from a C string pointer with validation.

Returns `None` if the string is invalid. This is safe to call from C code as it never panics on invalid input.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#safety-1)
Safety

*   `ptr` must be a valid pointer to a null-terminated C string.

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#157)

#### pub fn [len](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.len-1)
(&self) -> [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)

Returns the length of `self`.

This length is in bytes, not [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s or graphemes. In other words, it might not be what a human considers the length of the string.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples)
Examples

    let len = "foo".len();
    assert_eq!(3, len);
    
    assert_eq!("ƒoo".len(), 4); // fancy f!
    assert_eq!("ƒoo".chars().count(), 3);

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#177)

#### pub fn [is\_empty](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.is_empty-1)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if `self` has a length of zero bytes.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-1)
Examples

    let s = "";
    assert!(s.is_empty());
    
    let s = "not empty";
    assert!(!s.is_empty());

1.9.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#377)

#### pub fn [is\_char\_boundary](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.is_char_boundary)
(&self, index: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Checks that `index`\-th byte is the first byte in a UTF-8 code point sequence or the end of the string.

The start and end of the string (when `index == self.len()`) are considered to be boundaries.

Returns `false` if `index` is greater than `self.len()`.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-2)
Examples

    let s = "Löwe 老虎 Léopard";
    assert!(s.is_char_boundary(0));
    // start of `老`
    assert!(s.is_char_boundary(6));
    assert!(s.is_char_boundary(s.len()));
    
    // second byte of `ö`
    assert!(!s.is_char_boundary(2));
    
    // third byte of `老`
    assert!(!s.is_char_boundary(8));

1.91.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#426)

#### pub fn [floor\_char\_boundary](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.floor_char_boundary)
(&self, index: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)

Finds the closest `x` not exceeding `index` where [`is_char_boundary(x)`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.is_char_boundary "method str::is_char_boundary")
 is `true`.

This method can help you truncate a string so that it’s still valid UTF-8, but doesn’t exceed a given number of bytes. Note that this is done purely at the character level and can still visually split graphemes, even though the underlying characters aren’t split. For example, the emoji 🧑‍🔬 (scientist) could be split so that the string only includes 🧑 (person) instead.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-3)
Examples

    let s = "❤️🧡💛💚💙💜";
    assert_eq!(s.len(), 26);
    assert!(!s.is_char_boundary(13));
    
    let closest = s.floor_char_boundary(13);
    assert_eq!(closest, 10);
    assert_eq!(&s[..closest], "❤️🧡");

1.91.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#469)

#### pub fn [ceil\_char\_boundary](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.ceil_char_boundary)
(&self, index: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)

Finds the closest `x` not below `index` where [`is_char_boundary(x)`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.is_char_boundary "method str::is_char_boundary")
 is `true`.

If `index` is greater than the length of the string, this returns the length of the string.

This method is the natural complement to [`floor_char_boundary`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.floor_char_boundary "method str::floor_char_boundary")
. See that method for more details.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-4)
Examples

    let s = "❤️🧡💛💚💙💜";
    assert_eq!(s.len(), 26);
    assert!(!s.is_char_boundary(13));
    
    let closest = s.ceil_char_boundary(13);
    assert_eq!(closest, 14);
    assert_eq!(&s[..closest], "❤️🧡💛");

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#502)

#### pub fn [as\_bytes](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.as_bytes)
(&self) -> &\[[u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
\] [ⓘ](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#)

Converts a string slice to a byte slice. To convert the byte slice back into a string slice, use the [`from_utf8`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/converts/fn.from_utf8.html "fn core::str::converts::from_utf8")
 function.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-5)
Examples

    let bytes = "bors".as_bytes();
    assert_eq!(b"bors", bytes);

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#578)

#### pub fn [as\_ptr](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.as_ptr-1)
(&self) -> [\*const](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.pointer.html)
 [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)

Converts a string slice to a raw pointer.

As string slices are a slice of bytes, the raw pointer points to a [`u8`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html "primitive u8")
. This pointer will be pointing to the first byte of the string slice.

The caller must ensure that the returned pointer is never written to. If you need to mutate the contents of the string slice, use [`as_mut_ptr`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.as_mut_ptr "method str::as_mut_ptr")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-6)
Examples

    let s = "Hello";
    let ptr = s.as_ptr();

1.20.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#622)

#### pub fn [get](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.get)
<I>(&self, i: I) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<&<I as [SliceIndex](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/slice/index/trait.SliceIndex.html "trait core::slice::index::SliceIndex")
<[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>::[Output](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/slice/index/trait.SliceIndex.html#associatedtype.Output "type core::slice::index::SliceIndex::Output")
\>

where I: [SliceIndex](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/slice/index/trait.SliceIndex.html "trait core::slice::index::SliceIndex")
<[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>,

Returns a subslice of `str`.

This is the non-panicking alternative to indexing the `str`. Returns [`None`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html#variant.None "variant core::option::Option::None")
 whenever equivalent indexing operation would panic.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-7)
Examples

    let v = String::from("🗻∈🌏");
    
    assert_eq!(Some("🗻"), v.get(0..4));
    
    // indices not on UTF-8 sequence boundaries
    assert!(v.get(1..).is_none());
    assert!(v.get(..8).is_none());
    
    // out of bounds
    assert!(v.get(..42).is_none());

1.20.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#687)

#### pub unsafe fn [get\_unchecked](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.get_unchecked)
<I>(&self, i: I) -> &<I as [SliceIndex](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/slice/index/trait.SliceIndex.html "trait core::slice::index::SliceIndex")
<[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>::[Output](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/slice/index/trait.SliceIndex.html#associatedtype.Output "type core::slice::index::SliceIndex::Output")

where I: [SliceIndex](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/slice/index/trait.SliceIndex.html "trait core::slice::index::SliceIndex")
<[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>,

Returns an unchecked subslice of `str`.

This is the unchecked alternative to indexing the `str`.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#safety-2)
Safety

Callers of this function are responsible that these preconditions are satisfied:

*   The starting index must not exceed the ending index;
*   Indexes must be within bounds of the original slice;
*   Indexes must lie on UTF-8 sequence boundaries.

Failing that, the returned string slice may reference invalid memory or violate the invariants communicated by the `str` type.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-8)
Examples

    let v = "🗻∈🌏";
    unsafe {
        assert_eq!("🗻", v.get_unchecked(0..4));
        assert_eq!("∈", v.get_unchecked(4..7));
        assert_eq!("🌏", v.get_unchecked(7..11));
    }

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#773)

#### pub unsafe fn [slice\_unchecked](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.slice_unchecked)
(&self, begin: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
, end: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

👎Deprecated since 1.29.0:

use `get_unchecked(begin..end)` instead

Creates a string slice from another string slice, bypassing safety checks.

This is generally not recommended, use with caution! For a safe alternative see [`str`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html "primitive str")
 and [`Index`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/index/trait.Index.html "trait core::ops::index::Index")
.

This new slice goes from `begin` to `end`, including `begin` but excluding `end`.

To get a mutable string slice instead, see the [`slice_mut_unchecked`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.slice_mut_unchecked "method str::slice_mut_unchecked")
 method.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#safety-3)
Safety

Callers of this function are responsible that three preconditions are satisfied:

*   `begin` must not exceed `end`.
*   `begin` and `end` must be byte positions within the string slice.
*   `begin` and `end` must lie on UTF-8 sequence boundaries.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-9)
Examples

    let s = "Löwe 老虎 Léopard";
    
    unsafe {
        assert_eq!("Löwe 老虎 Léopard", s.slice_unchecked(0, 21));
    }
    
    let s = "Hello, world!";
    
    unsafe {
        assert_eq!("world", s.slice_unchecked(7, 12));
    }

1.4.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#847)

#### pub fn [split\_at](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.split_at)
(&self, mid: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> (&[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
)

Divides one string slice into two at an index.

The argument, `mid`, should be a byte offset from the start of the string. It must also be on the boundary of a UTF-8 code point.

The two slices returned go from the start of the string slice to `mid`, and from `mid` to the end of the string slice.

To get mutable string slices instead, see the [`split_at_mut`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.split_at_mut "method str::split_at_mut")
 method.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#panics-1)
Panics

Panics if `mid` is not on a UTF-8 code point boundary, or if it is past the end of the last code point of the string slice. For a non-panicking alternative see [`split_at_checked`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.split_at_checked "method str::split_at_checked")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-10)
Examples

    let s = "Per Martin-Löf";
    
    let (first, last) = s.split_at(3);
    
    assert_eq!("Per", first);
    assert_eq!(" Martin-Löf", last);

1.80.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#928)

#### pub fn [split\_at\_checked](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.split_at_checked)
(&self, mid: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<(&[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
)>

Divides one string slice into two at an index.

The argument, `mid`, should be a valid byte offset from the start of the string. It must also be on the boundary of a UTF-8 code point. The method returns `None` if that’s not the case.

The two slices returned go from the start of the string slice to `mid`, and from `mid` to the end of the string slice.

To get mutable string slices instead, see the [`split_at_mut_checked`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.split_at_mut_checked "method str::split_at_mut_checked")
 method.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-11)
Examples

    let s = "Per Martin-Löf";
    
    let (first, last) = s.split_at_checked(3).unwrap();
    assert_eq!("Per", first);
    assert_eq!(" Martin-Löf", last);
    
    assert_eq!(None, s.split_at_checked(13));  // Inside “ö”
    assert_eq!(None, s.split_at_checked(16));  // Beyond the string length

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#1066)

#### pub fn [chars](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.chars)
(&self) -> [Chars](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.Chars.html "struct core::str::iter::Chars")
<'\_>

Returns an iterator over the [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s of a string slice.

As a string slice consists of valid UTF-8, we can iterate through a string slice by [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
. This method returns such an iterator.

It’s important to remember that [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
 represents a Unicode Scalar Value, and might not match your idea of what a ‘character’ is. Iteration over grapheme clusters may be what you actually want. This functionality is not provided by Rust’s standard library, check crates.io instead.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-12)
Examples

Basic usage:

    let word = "goodbye";
    
    let count = word.chars().count();
    assert_eq!(7, count);
    
    let mut chars = word.chars();
    
    assert_eq!(Some('g'), chars.next());
    assert_eq!(Some('o'), chars.next());
    assert_eq!(Some('o'), chars.next());
    assert_eq!(Some('d'), chars.next());
    assert_eq!(Some('b'), chars.next());
    assert_eq!(Some('y'), chars.next());
    assert_eq!(Some('e'), chars.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, chars.next());

Remember, [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s might not match your intuition about characters:

    let y = "y̆";
    
    let mut chars = y.chars();
    
    assert_eq!(Some('y'), chars.next()); // not 'y̆'
    assert_eq!(Some('\u{0306}'), chars.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, chars.next());

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#1123)

#### pub fn [char\_indices](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.char_indices)
(&self) -> [CharIndices](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.CharIndices.html "struct core::str::iter::CharIndices")
<'\_>

Returns an iterator over the [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s of a string slice, and their positions.

As a string slice consists of valid UTF-8, we can iterate through a string slice by [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
. This method returns an iterator of both these [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s, as well as their byte positions.

The iterator yields tuples. The position is first, the [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
 is second.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-13)
Examples

Basic usage:

    let word = "goodbye";
    
    let count = word.char_indices().count();
    assert_eq!(7, count);
    
    let mut char_indices = word.char_indices();
    
    assert_eq!(Some((0, 'g')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((1, 'o')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((2, 'o')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((3, 'd')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((4, 'b')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((5, 'y')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((6, 'e')), char_indices.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, char_indices.next());

Remember, [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s might not match your intuition about characters:

    let yes = "y̆es";
    
    let mut char_indices = yes.char_indices();
    
    assert_eq!(Some((0, 'y')), char_indices.next()); // not (0, 'y̆')
    assert_eq!(Some((1, '\u{0306}')), char_indices.next());
    
    // note the 3 here - the previous character took up two bytes
    assert_eq!(Some((3, 'e')), char_indices.next());
    assert_eq!(Some((4, 's')), char_indices.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, char_indices.next());

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#1146)

#### pub fn [bytes](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.bytes)
(&self) -> [Bytes](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.Bytes.html "struct core::str::iter::Bytes")
<'\_>

Returns an iterator over the bytes of a string slice.

As a string slice consists of a sequence of bytes, we can iterate through a string slice by byte. This method returns such an iterator.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-14)
Examples

    let mut bytes = "bors".bytes();
    
    assert_eq!(Some(b'b'), bytes.next());
    assert_eq!(Some(b'o'), bytes.next());
    assert_eq!(Some(b'r'), bytes.next());
    assert_eq!(Some(b's'), bytes.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, bytes.next());

1.1.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#1198)

#### pub fn [split\_whitespace](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.split_whitespace)
(&self) -> [SplitWhitespace](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.SplitWhitespace.html "struct core::str::iter::SplitWhitespace")
<'\_>

Splits a string slice by whitespace.

The iterator returned will return string slices that are sub-slices of the original string slice, separated by any amount of whitespace.

‘Whitespace’ is defined according to the terms of the Unicode Derived Core Property `White_Space`. If you only want to split on ASCII whitespace instead, use [`split_ascii_whitespace`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.split_ascii_whitespace "method str::split_ascii_whitespace")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-15)
Examples

Basic usage:

    let mut iter = "A few words".split_whitespace();
    
    assert_eq!(Some("A"), iter.next());
    assert_eq!(Some("few"), iter.next());
    assert_eq!(Some("words"), iter.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, iter.next());

All kinds of whitespace are considered:

    let mut iter = " Mary   had\ta\u{2009}little  \n\t lamb".split_whitespace();
    assert_eq!(Some("Mary"), iter.next());
    assert_eq!(Some("had"), iter.next());
    assert_eq!(Some("a"), iter.next());
    assert_eq!(Some("little"), iter.next());
    assert_eq!(Some("lamb"), iter.next());
    
    assert_eq!(None, iter.next());

If the string is empty or all whitespace, the iterator yields no string slices:

    assert_eq!("".split_whitespace().next(), None);
    assert_eq!("   ".split_whitespace().next(), None);

1.34.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#1249)

#### pub fn [split\_ascii\_whitespace](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.split_ascii_whitespace)
(&self) -> [SplitAsciiWhitespace](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.SplitAsciiWhitespace.html "struct core::str::iter::SplitAsciiWhitespace")
<'\_>

Splits a string slice by ASCII whitespace.

The iterator returned will return string slices that are sub-slices of the original string slice, separated by any amount of ASCII whitespace.

This uses the same definition as [`char::is_ascii_whitespace`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html#method.is_ascii_whitespace "method char::is_ascii_whitespace")
