Returns a string slice with all suffixes that match a pattern repeatedly removed.

The [pattern](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/pattern/index.html "mod core::str::pattern")
 can be a `&str`, [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
, a slice of [`char`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html "primitive char")
s, or a function or closure that determines if a character matches.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#text-directionality-7)
Text directionality

A string is a sequence of bytes. ‘Right’ in this context means the last position of that byte string; for a language like Arabic or Hebrew which are ‘right to left’ rather than ‘left to right’, this will be the _left_ side, not the right.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-51)
Examples

Simple patterns:

    assert_eq!("11foo1bar11".trim_right_matches('1'), "11foo1bar");
    assert_eq!("123foo1bar123".trim_right_matches(char::is_numeric), "123foo1bar");
    
    let x: &[_] = &['1', '2'];
    assert_eq!("12foo1bar12".trim_right_matches(x), "12foo1bar");

A more complex pattern, using a closure:

    assert_eq!("1fooX".trim_right_matches(|c| c == '1' || c == 'X'), "1foo");

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2765)

#### pub fn [parse](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.parse)
<F>(&self) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<F, <F as [FromStr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/traits/trait.FromStr.html "trait core::str::traits::FromStr")
\>::[Err](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/traits/trait.FromStr.html#associatedtype.Err "type core::str::traits::FromStr::Err")
\>

where F: [FromStr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/traits/trait.FromStr.html "trait core::str::traits::FromStr")
,

Parses this string slice into another type.

Because `parse` is so general, it can cause problems with type inference. As such, `parse` is one of the few times you’ll see the syntax affectionately known as the ‘turbofish’: `::<>`. This helps the inference algorithm understand specifically which type you’re trying to parse into.

`parse` can parse into any type that implements the [`FromStr`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/traits/trait.FromStr.html "trait core::str::traits::FromStr")
 trait.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#errors-2)
Errors

Will return [`Err`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/traits/trait.FromStr.html#associatedtype.Err "associated type core::str::traits::FromStr::Err")
 if it’s not possible to parse this string slice into the desired type.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-52)
Examples

Basic usage:

    let four: u32 = "4".parse().unwrap();
    
    assert_eq!(4, four);

Using the ‘turbofish’ instead of annotating `four`:

    let four = "4".parse::<u32>();
    
    assert_eq!(Ok(4), four);

Failing to parse:

    let nope = "j".parse::<u32>();
    
    assert!(nope.is_err());

1.23.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2786)

#### pub fn [is\_ascii](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.is_ascii)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Checks if all characters in this string are within the ASCII range.

An empty string returns `true`.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-53)
Examples

    let ascii = "hello!\n";
    let non_ascii = "Grüße, Jürgen ❤";
    
    assert!(ascii.is_ascii());
    assert!(!non_ascii.is_ascii());

[Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2798)

#### pub fn [as\_ascii](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.as_ascii)
(&self) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<&\[[AsciiChar](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ascii/ascii_char/enum.AsciiChar.html "enum core::ascii::ascii_char::AsciiChar")\
\]>

🔬This is a nightly-only experimental API. (`ascii_char`)

If this string slice [`is_ascii`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.is_ascii "method str::is_ascii")
, returns it as a slice of [ASCII characters](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ascii/ascii_char/enum.AsciiChar.html "enum core::ascii::ascii_char::AsciiChar")
, otherwise returns `None`.

[Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2812)

#### pub unsafe fn [as\_ascii\_unchecked](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.as_ascii_unchecked)
(&self) -> &\[[AsciiChar](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ascii/ascii_char/enum.AsciiChar.html "enum core::ascii::ascii_char::AsciiChar")\
\]

🔬This is a nightly-only experimental API. (`ascii_char`)

Converts this string slice into a slice of [ASCII characters](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ascii/ascii_char/enum.AsciiChar.html "enum core::ascii::ascii_char::AsciiChar")
, without checking whether they are valid.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#safety-4)
Safety

Every character in this string must be ASCII, or else this is UB.

1.23.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2840)

#### pub fn [eq\_ignore\_ascii\_case](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.eq_ignore_ascii_case)
(&self, other: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Checks that two strings are an ASCII case-insensitive match.

Same as `to_ascii_lowercase(a) == to_ascii_lowercase(b)`, but without allocating and copying temporaries.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-54)
Examples

    assert!("Ferris".eq_ignore_ascii_case("FERRIS"));
    assert!("Ferrös".eq_ignore_ascii_case("FERRöS"));
    assert!(!"Ferrös".eq_ignore_ascii_case("FERRÖS"));

1.80.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2919)

#### pub fn [trim\_ascii\_start](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.trim_ascii_start)
(&self) -> &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

Returns a string slice with leading ASCII whitespace removed.

‘Whitespace’ refers to the definition used by [`u8::is_ascii_whitespace`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html#method.is_ascii_whitespace "method u8::is_ascii_whitespace")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-55)
Examples

    assert_eq!(" \t \u{3000}hello world\n".trim_ascii_start(), "\u{3000}hello world\n");
    assert_eq!("  ".trim_ascii_start(), "");
    assert_eq!("".trim_ascii_start(), "");

1.80.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2944)

#### pub fn [trim\_ascii\_end](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.trim_ascii_end)
(&self) -> &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

Returns a string slice with trailing ASCII whitespace removed.

‘Whitespace’ refers to the definition used by [`u8::is_ascii_whitespace`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html#method.is_ascii_whitespace "method u8::is_ascii_whitespace")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-56)
Examples

    assert_eq!("\r hello world\u{3000}\n ".trim_ascii_end(), "\r hello world\u{3000}");
    assert_eq!("  ".trim_ascii_end(), "");
    assert_eq!("".trim_ascii_end(), "");

1.80.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#2970)

#### pub fn [trim\_ascii](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.trim_ascii)
(&self) -> &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

Returns a string slice with leading and trailing ASCII whitespace removed.

‘Whitespace’ refers to the definition used by [`u8::is_ascii_whitespace`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html#method.is_ascii_whitespace "method u8::is_ascii_whitespace")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-57)
Examples

    assert_eq!("\r hello world\n ".trim_ascii(), "hello world");
    assert_eq!("  ".trim_ascii(), "");
    assert_eq!("".trim_ascii(), "");

1.34.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#3013)

#### pub fn [escape\_debug](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.escape_debug)
(&self) -> [EscapeDebug](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.EscapeDebug.html "struct core::str::iter::EscapeDebug")
<'\_>

Returns an iterator that escapes each char in `self` with [`char::escape_debug`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html#method.escape_debug "method char::escape_debug")
.

Note: only extended grapheme codepoints that begin the string will be escaped.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-58)
Examples

As an iterator:

    for c in "❤\n!".escape_debug() {
        print!("{c}");
    }
    println!();

Using `println!` directly:

    println!("{}", "❤\n!".escape_debug());

Both are equivalent to:

    println!("❤\\n!");

Using `to_string`:

    assert_eq!("❤\n!".escape_debug().to_string(), "❤\\n!");

1.34.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#3059)

#### pub fn [escape\_default](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.escape_default)
(&self) -> [EscapeDefault](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.EscapeDefault.html "struct core::str::iter::EscapeDefault")
<'\_>

Returns an iterator that escapes each char in `self` with [`char::escape_default`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html#method.escape_default "method char::escape_default")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-59)
Examples

As an iterator:

    for c in "❤\n!".escape_default() {
        print!("{c}");
    }
    println!();

Using `println!` directly:

    println!("{}", "❤\n!".escape_default());

Both are equivalent to:

    println!("\\u{{2764}}\\n!");

Using `to_string`:

    assert_eq!("❤\n!".escape_default().to_string(), "\\u{2764}\\n!");

1.34.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#3097)

#### pub fn [escape\_unicode](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.escape_unicode)
(&self) -> [EscapeUnicode](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/iter/struct.EscapeUnicode.html "struct core::str::iter::EscapeUnicode")
<'\_>

Returns an iterator that escapes each char in `self` with [`char::escape_unicode`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.char.html#method.escape_unicode "method char::escape_unicode")
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-60)
Examples

As an iterator:

    for c in "❤\n!".escape_unicode() {
        print!("{c}");
    }
    println!();

Using `println!` directly:

    println!("{}", "❤\n!".escape_unicode());

Both are equivalent to:

    println!("\\u{{2764}}\\u{{a}}\\u{{21}}");

Using `to_string`:

    assert_eq!("❤\n!".escape_unicode().to_string(), "\\u{2764}\\u{a}\\u{21}");

[Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#3130)

#### pub fn [substr\_range](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.substr_range)
(&self, substr: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[Range](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/range/struct.Range.html "struct core::ops::range::Range")
<[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
\>>

🔬This is a nightly-only experimental API. (`substr_range`)

Returns the range that a substring points to.

Returns `None` if `substr` does not point within `self`.

Unlike [`str::find`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.find "method str::find")
, **this does not search through the string**. Instead, it uses pointer arithmetic to find where in the string `substr` is derived from.

This is useful for extending [`str::split`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.split "method str::split")
 and similar methods.

Note that this method may return false positives (typically either `Some(0..0)` or `Some(self.len()..self.len())`) if `substr` is a zero-length `str` that points at the beginning or end of another, independent, `str`.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-61)
Examples

    #![feature(substr_range)]
    
    let data = "a, b, b, a";
    let mut iter = data.split(", ").map(|s| data.substr_range(s).unwrap());
    
    assert_eq!(iter.next(), Some(0..1));
    assert_eq!(iter.next(), Some(3..4));
    assert_eq!(iter.next(), Some(6..7));
    assert_eq!(iter.next(), Some(9..10));

[Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/str/mod.rs.html#3141)

#### pub fn [as\_str](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.as_str-1)
(&self) -> &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

🔬This is a nightly-only experimental API. (`str_as_str`)

Returns the same string as a string slice `&str`.

This method is redundant when used directly on `&str`, but it helps dereferencing other string-like types to string slices, for example references to `Box<str>` or `Arc<str>`.

1.0.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#268)

#### pub fn [replace](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.replace)
<P>(&self, from: P, to: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

where P: [Pattern](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/pattern/trait.Pattern.html "trait core::str::pattern::Pattern")
,

Replaces all matches of a pattern with another string.

`replace` creates a new [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
, and copies the data from this string slice into it. While doing so, it attempts to find matches of a pattern. If it finds any, it replaces them with the replacement string slice.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-62)
Examples

    let s = "this is old";
    
    assert_eq!("this is new", s.replace("old", "new"));
    assert_eq!("than an old", s.replace("is", "an"));

When the pattern doesn’t match, it returns this string slice as [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
:

    let s = "this is old";
    assert_eq!(s, s.replace("cookie monster", "little lamb"));

1.16.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#323)

#### pub fn [replacen](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.replacen)
<P>(&self, pat: P, to: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, count: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

where P: [Pattern](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/str/pattern/trait.Pattern.html "trait core::str::pattern::Pattern")
,

Replaces first N matches of a pattern with another string.

`replacen` creates a new [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
, and copies the data from this string slice into it. While doing so, it attempts to find matches of a pattern. If it finds any, it replaces them with the replacement string slice at most `count` times.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-63)
Examples

    let s = "foo foo 123 foo";
    assert_eq!("new new 123 foo", s.replacen("foo", "new", 2));
    assert_eq!("faa fao 123 foo", s.replacen('o', "a", 3));
    assert_eq!("foo foo new23 foo", s.replacen(char::is_numeric, "new", 1));

When the pattern doesn’t match, it returns this string slice as [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
:

    let s = "this is old";
    assert_eq!(s, s.replacen("cookie monster", "little lamb", 10));

1.2.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#380)

#### pub fn [to\_lowercase](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.to_lowercase)
(&self) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

Returns the lowercase equivalent of this string slice, as a new [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
.

‘Lowercase’ is defined according to the terms of the Unicode Derived Core Property `Lowercase`.

Since some characters can expand into multiple characters when changing the case, this function returns a [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
 instead of modifying the parameter in-place.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-64)
Examples

Basic usage:

    let s = "HELLO";
    
    assert_eq!("hello", s.to_lowercase());

A tricky example, with sigma:

    let sigma = "Σ";
    
    assert_eq!("σ", sigma.to_lowercase());
    
    // but at the end of a word, it's ς, not σ:
    let odysseus = "ὈΔΥΣΣΕΎΣ";
    
    assert_eq!("ὀδυσσεύς", odysseus.to_lowercase());

Languages without case are not changed:

    let new_year = "农历新年";
    
    assert_eq!(new_year, new_year.to_lowercase());

1.2.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#465)

#### pub fn [to\_uppercase](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.to_uppercase)
(&self) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

Returns the uppercase equivalent of this string slice, as a new [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
.

‘Uppercase’ is defined according to the terms of the Unicode Derived Core Property `Uppercase`.

Since some characters can expand into multiple characters when changing the case, this function returns a [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
 instead of modifying the parameter in-place.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-65)
Examples

Basic usage:

    let s = "hello";
    
    assert_eq!("HELLO", s.to_uppercase());

Scripts without case are not changed:

    let new_year = "农历新年";
    
    assert_eq!(new_year, new_year.to_uppercase());

One character can become multiple:

    let s = "tschüß";
    
    assert_eq!("TSCHÜSS", s.to_uppercase());

1.16.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#529)

#### pub fn [repeat](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.repeat)
(&self, n: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

Creates a new [`String`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
 by repeating a string `n` times.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#panics-2)
Panics

This function will panic if the capacity would overflow.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-66)
Examples

Basic usage:

    assert_eq!("abc".repeat(4), String::from("abcabcabcabc"));

A panic upon overflow:

[ⓘ](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html# "This example panics")

    // this will panic at runtime
    let huge = "0123456789abcdef".repeat(usize::MAX);

1.23.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#559)

#### pub fn [to\_ascii\_uppercase](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.to_ascii_uppercase)
(&self) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

Returns a copy of this string where each character is mapped to its ASCII upper case equivalent.

ASCII letters ‘a’ to ‘z’ are mapped to ‘A’ to ‘Z’, but non-ASCII letters are unchanged.

To uppercase the value in-place, use [`make_ascii_uppercase`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.make_ascii_uppercase "method str::make_ascii_uppercase")
.

To uppercase ASCII characters in addition to non-ASCII characters, use [`to_uppercase`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.to_uppercase)
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-67)
Examples

    let s = "Grüße, Jürgen ❤";
    
    assert_eq!("GRüßE, JüRGEN ❤", s.to_ascii_uppercase());

1.23.0 · [Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/alloc/str.rs.html#591)

#### pub fn [to\_ascii\_lowercase](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.to_ascii_lowercase)
(&self) -> [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")

Returns a copy of this string where each character is mapped to its ASCII lower case equivalent.

ASCII letters ‘A’ to ‘Z’ are mapped to ‘a’ to ‘z’, but non-ASCII letters are unchanged.

To lowercase the value in-place, use [`make_ascii_lowercase`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html#method.make_ascii_lowercase "method str::make_ascii_lowercase")
.

To lowercase ASCII characters in addition to non-ASCII characters, use [`to_lowercase`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.to_lowercase)
.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#examples-68)
Examples

    let s = "Grüße, Jürgen ❤";
    
    assert_eq!("grüße, jürgen ❤", s.to_ascii_lowercase());

### impl [Borrow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/borrow/trait.Borrow.html "trait core::borrow::Borrow")

#### fn [default](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/default/trait.Default.html#tymethod.default)
() -> Self

Creates an empty [`StackStr`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html "struct nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr")
 with length 0.

Note: While [`StackStr::new`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.new "associated function nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr::new")
 rejects empty strings, `default()` creates an empty placeholder. Use [`is_empty`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/stack_str/struct.StackStr.html#method.is_empty "method nautilus_core::stack_str::StackStr::is_empty")

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")

,

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

# nautilus_execution::engine - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/engine/index.html#main-content)

[Module engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/engine/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/index.html)

Module engine Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_execution/engine/mod.rs.html#16-1941)

Expand description

Provides a generic `ExecutionEngine` for all environments.

The execution engines primary responsibility is to orchestrate interactions between the `ExecutionClient` instances, and the rest of the platform. This includes sending commands to, and receiving events from, the trading venue endpoints via its registered execution clients.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/engine/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ExecutionEngine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_execution/engine/struct.ExecutionEngine.html "struct nautilus_execution::engine::ExecutionEngine")

Central execution engine responsible for orchestrating order routing and execution.

# nautilus_binance::futures - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#main-content)

[Module futures](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_binance](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/index.html)

Module futures Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_binance/futures/mod.rs.html#16-46)

Expand description

Binance Futures adapter components.

This module provides HTTP and WebSocket clients for Binance Futures:

*   **USD-M Futures** (`fapi.binance.com`) - USDT-margined perpetual contracts
*   **COIN-M Futures** (`dapi.binance.com`) - Coin-margined perpetual contracts

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#websocket-streams)
WebSocket Streams

Unlike Spot which uses SBE binary encoding, Futures uses standard JSON WebSocket streams:

*   `<symbol>@trade` - Real-time trade data
*   `<symbol>@depth` - Order book updates (diff)
*   `<symbol>@depth@100ms` - Order book updates (100ms frequency)
*   `<symbol>@markPrice` - Mark price updates
*   `<symbol>@kline_<interval>` - Kline/candlestick updates

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#authentication)
Authentication

*   Public streams: No authentication required
*   User data streams: Requires listen key (obtained via REST API)

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#reexports)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use data::[BinanceFuturesDataClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/data/struct.BinanceFuturesDataClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::futures::data::BinanceFuturesDataClient") ;`

`pub use execution::[BinanceFuturesExecutionClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/execution/struct.BinanceFuturesExecutionClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::futures::execution::BinanceFuturesExecutionClient") ;`

`pub use http::client::[BinanceFuturesHttpClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/http/client/struct.BinanceFuturesHttpClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::futures::http::client::BinanceFuturesHttpClient") ;`

`pub use websocket::client::[BinanceFuturesWebSocketClient](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/websocket/client/struct.BinanceFuturesWebSocketClient.html "struct nautilus_binance::futures::websocket::client::BinanceFuturesWebSocketClient") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/data/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::futures::data")

Live market data client implementation for the Binance Futures adapter.

[execution](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/execution/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::futures::execution")

Live execution client implementation for the Binance Futures adapter.

[http](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_binance/futures/http/index.html "mod nautilus_binance::futures::http")

Binance Futures HTTP client with JSON encoding.

Module throttler Copy item path
===============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_common/throttler.rs.html#16-850)

Expand description

Message throttling and rate limiting functionality.

This module provides throttling capabilities to control the rate of message processing and prevent system overload. The throttler can buffer, drop, or delay messages based on configured rate limits and time intervals.

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/throttler/index.html#structs)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[RateLimit](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/throttler/struct.RateLimit.html "struct nautilus_common::throttler::RateLimit")

Represents a throttling limit per interval.

[Throttler](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/throttler/struct.Throttler.html "struct nautilus_common::throttler::Throttler")

Throttler rate limits messages by dropping or buffering them.

Functions[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/throttler/index.html#functions)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[throttler\_resume](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/throttler/fn.throttler_resume.html "fn nautilus_common::throttler::throttler_resume")

Sets throttler to resume sending messages.

    pub trait ClientConfig: Debug {
        // Required method
        fn as_any(&self) -> &dyn Any;
    }

Expand description

Configuration for creating client instances.

This trait allows different client types to provide their configuration in a type-safe manner while still being usable in generic factory contexts.

# nautilus_bybit::error - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/index.html#main-content)

[Module error](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_bybit](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/index.html)

Module error Copy item path
===========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_bybit/error.rs.html#16-557)

Expand description

Unified error handling for the Bybit adapter.

This module provides a comprehensive error taxonomy that distinguishes between retryable, non-retryable, and fatal errors, with proper context preservation for debugging and operational monitoring.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/index.html#reexports)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::http::error::[BybitHttpError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/http/error/enum.BybitHttpError.html "enum nautilus_bybit::http::error::BybitHttpError") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::error::[BybitWsError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/websocket/error/enum.BybitWsError.html "enum nautilus_bybit::websocket::error::BybitWsError") ;`

`pub use crate::websocket::error::[BybitWsResult](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/websocket/error/type.BybitWsResult.html "type nautilus_bybit::websocket::error::BybitWsResult") ;`

Enums[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/index.html#enums)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[BybitError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/enum.BybitError.html "enum nautilus_bybit::error::BybitError")

The main error type for all Bybit adapter operations.

[BybitFatalError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/enum.BybitFatalError.html "enum nautilus_bybit::error::BybitFatalError")

Fatal errors that require manual intervention.

[BybitNonRetryableError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/enum.BybitNonRetryableError.html "enum nautilus_bybit::error::BybitNonRetryableError")

Errors that should not be retried.

[BybitRetryableError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_bybit/error/enum.BybitRetryableError.html "enum nautilus_bybit::error::BybitRetryableError")

Errors that should be retried with appropriate backoff.

# ExecutionClientFactory in nautilus_system::factories - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.ExecutionClientFactory.html#main-content)

[ExecutionClientFactory](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.ExecutionClientFactory.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_system](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html)
::[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/index.html)

Trait ExecutionClientFactory Copy item path
===========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/factories.rs.html#63-81)

    pub trait ExecutionClientFactory: Debug {
        // Required methods
        fn create(
            &self,
            name: &str,
            config: &dyn ClientConfig,
            cache: Rc<RefCell<Cache>>,
        ) -> Result<Box<dyn ExecutionClient>>;
        fn name(&self) -> &str;
        fn config_type(&self) -> &str;
    }

Expand description

Factory trait for creating execution client instances.

Implementations of this trait should create specific execution client types (e.g., Binance, Bybit, Interactive Brokers) based on the provided configuration.

Module signing Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_cryptography/signing.rs.html#16-186)

Functions[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/signing/index.html#functions)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ed25519\_signature](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/signing/fn.ed25519_signature.html "fn nautilus_cryptography::signing::ed25519_signature")

Signs `data` using Ed25519 with the provided private key seed.

[hmac\_signature](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/signing/fn.hmac_signature.html "fn nautilus_cryptography::signing::hmac_signature")

Generates an HMAC-SHA256 signature for the given data using the provided secret.

[rsa\_signature](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_cryptography/signing/fn.rsa_signature.html "fn nautilus_cryptography::signing::rsa_signature")

Signs `data` using RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 SHA-256 with the provided private key in PEM format.

# nautilus_model::currencies - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/currencies/index.html#main-content)

[Module currencies](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/currencies/index.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_model](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/index.html)

Module currencies Copy item path
================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_model/currencies.rs.html#16-1325)

Expand description

Common `Currency` constants.

Precision and metadata references:

*   ISO 4217 Maintenance Agency dataset ([https://github.com/datasets/currency-codes](https://github.com/datasets/currency-codes)
    ): authoritative alphabetic codes, numeric codes, and minor units for fiat and commodity-backed entries.
*   Cardano ledger documentation ([https://docs.cardano.org/native-tokens/understanding-assets](https://docs.cardano.org/native-tokens/understanding-assets)
    ): 1 ADA = 1,000,000 lovelace, underpinning the six-decimal crypto precision we retain.
*   XRPL documentation on drops ([https://xrpl.org/xrp-ledger-tokens.html#drops-and-xrp](https://xrpl.org/xrp-ledger-tokens.html#drops-and-xrp)
    ): 1 XRP = 1,000,000 drops, confirming the six-decimal allowance for XRP.
*   Tezos protocol reference ([https://tezos.gitlab.io/active/numismatics.html](https://tezos.gitlab.io/active/numismatics.html)
    ): 1 tez = 1,000,000 mutez, informing the six-decimal precision for XTZ.
*   Stablecoin contract metadata on Etherscan for USDC, USDP, and BRZ (e.g. [https://etherscan.io/token/0xa0b86991c6218b36c1d19d4a2e9eb0ce3606eb48#readContract](https://etherscan.io/token/0xa0b86991c6218b36c1d19d4a2e9eb0ce3606eb48#readContract)
    , [https://etherscan.io/token/0x8e870d67f660d95d5be530380d0ec0bd388289e1#readContract](https://etherscan.io/token/0x8e870d67f660d95d5be530380d0ec0bd388289e1#readContract)
    , [https://etherscan.io/token/0x01d33fd36ec67c6ada32cf36b31e88ee190b1839#readContract](https://etherscan.io/token/0x01d33fd36ec67c6ada32cf36b31e88ee190b1839#readContract)
    ): each exposes 6–18 on-chain decimals; we clamp to an 8-decimal internal default.

Statics[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/currencies/index.html#statics)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[CURRENCY\_MAP](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/currencies/static.CURRENCY_MAP.html "static nautilus_model::currencies::CURRENCY_MAP")

A map of built-in `Currency` constants.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_model/position.rs.html#16-3944)

Expand description

A `Position` for the trading domain model.

Represents an open or closed position a the market, tracking quantity, side, average prices, realized P&L, and the fill events that created and changed the position.

# nautilus_network::backoff - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/backoff/index.html#main-content)

[Module backoff](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/backoff/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_network](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html)

Module backoff Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_network/backoff.rs.html#16-463)

Expand description

Provides an implementation of an exponential backoff mechanism with jitter support. It is used for managing reconnection delays in the socket clients.

The backoff mechanism allows the delay to grow exponentially up to a configurable maximum, optionally applying random jitter to avoid synchronized reconnection storms. An “immediate first” flag is available so that the very first reconnect attempt can occur without any delay.

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/backoff/index.html#structs)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

# nautilus_common::defi - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/index.html#main-content)

[Module defi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/index.html#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/index.html)

Module defi Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_common/defi/mod.rs.html#16-39)

Expand description

DeFi (Decentralized Finance) integration for NautilusTrader.

This module provides centralized access to DeFi functionality throughout the common crate. DeFi support includes:

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/index.html#feature-flag)
Feature Flag
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All DeFi functionality requires the `defi` feature flag to be enabled:

    nautilus-common = { version = "0.x", features = ["defi"] }

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/index.html#reexports)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use switchboard::[get_defi_blocks_topic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/switchboard/fn.get_defi_blocks_topic.html "fn nautilus_common::defi::switchboard::get_defi_blocks_topic") ;`

`pub use switchboard::[get_defi_collect_topic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/switchboard/fn.get_defi_collect_topic.html "fn nautilus_common::defi::switchboard::get_defi_collect_topic") ;`

`pub use switchboard::[get_defi_flash_topic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/switchboard/fn.get_defi_flash_topic.html "fn nautilus_common::defi::switchboard::get_defi_flash_topic") ;`

`pub use switchboard::[get_defi_liquidity_topic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/switchboard/fn.get_defi_liquidity_topic.html "fn nautilus_common::defi::switchboard::get_defi_liquidity_topic") ;`

`pub use switchboard::[get_defi_pool_swaps_topic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/switchboard/fn.get_defi_pool_swaps_topic.html "fn nautilus_common::defi::switchboard::get_defi_pool_swaps_topic") ;`

`pub use switchboard::[get_defi_pool_topic](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/defi/switchboard/fn.get_defi_pool_topic.html "fn nautilus_common::defi::switchboard::get_defi_pool_topic") ;`

`pub use crate::messages::[defi](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_common/messages/defi/index.html "mod nautilus_common::messages::defi") ::*;`

Expand description

WebSocket client implementations for Kraken APIs.

This module provides WebSocket clients for real-time market data streams:

*   [`spot_v2`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_kraken/websocket/spot_v2/index.html "mod nautilus_kraken::websocket::spot_v2")
    : Kraken Spot WebSocket v2 API
*   [`futures`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_kraken/websocket/futures/index.html "mod nautilus_kraken::websocket::futures")
    : Kraken Futures WebSocket v1 API

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# DataClientFactory in nautilus_system::factories - Rust

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_system](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/index.html)
::[factories](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/index.html)

Trait DataClientFactory Copy item path
======================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/factories.rs.html#38-57)

    pub trait DataClientFactory: Debug {
        // Required methods
        fn create(
            &self,
            name: &str,
            config: &dyn ClientConfig,
            cache: Rc<RefCell<Cache>>,
            clock: Rc<RefCell<dyn Clock>>,
        ) -> Result<Box<dyn DataClient>>;
        fn name(&self) -> &str;
        fn config_type(&self) -> &str;
    }

Expand description

Factory trait for creating data client instances.

Implementations of this trait should create specific data client types (e.g., Binance, Bybit, Databento) based on the provided configuration.

Required Methods[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.DataClientFactory.html#required-methods)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/factories.rs.html#44-50)

#### fn [create](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.DataClientFactory.html#tymethod.create)
( &self, name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, config: &dyn [ClientConfig](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.ClientConfig.html "trait nautilus_system::factories::ClientConfig")
, cache: [Rc](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/rc/struct.Rc.html "struct alloc::rc::Rc")
<[RefCell](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/cell/struct.RefCell.html "struct core::cell::RefCell")
<Cache>>, clock: [Rc](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/rc/struct.Rc.html "struct alloc::rc::Rc")
<[RefCell](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/cell/struct.RefCell.html "struct core::cell::RefCell")
<dyn Clock>>, ) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/anyhow/1.0.102/anyhow/type.Result.html "type anyhow::Result")
<[Box](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct alloc::boxed::Box")
<dyn DataClient>>

Create a new data client instance.

##### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/factories/trait.DataClientFactory.html#errors)
Errors

Returns an error if client creation fails.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/factories.rs.html#53)

 events for use in order book and matching engine tests.

# nautilus_data::engine - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/engine/index.html#main-content)

[Module engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/engine/index.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_data](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/index.html)

Module engine Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_data/engine/mod.rs.html#16-1821)

Expand description

Provides a high-performance `DataEngine` for all environments.

The `DataEngine` is the central component of the entire data stack. The data engines primary responsibility is to orchestrate interactions between the `DataClient` instances, and the rest of the platform. This includes sending requests to, and receiving responses from, data endpoints via its registered data clients.

The engine employs a simple fan-in fan-out messaging pattern to execute `DataCommand` type messages, and process `DataResponse` messages or market data objects.

Alternative implementations can be written on top of the generic engine - which just need to override the `execute`, `process`, `send` and `receive` methods.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_data/engine/index.html#modules)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Message handler that maintains the `Pool` state stored in the global \[`Cache`\].

==============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_common/messages/mod.rs.html#16-61)

Expand description

Message types for system communication.

This module provides message types used for communication between different parts of the NautilusTrader system, including data requests, execution commands, and system control messages.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_serialization/sbe/mod.rs.html#16-30)

Expand description

Generic SBE (Simple Binary Encoding) decode utilities.

This module provides:

*   [`SbeCursor`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/cursor/struct.SbeCursor.html "struct nautilus_serialization::sbe::cursor::SbeCursor")
    : Zero-copy byte cursor with typed little-endian readers.
*   [`SbeDecodeError`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/error/enum.SbeDecodeError.html "enum nautilus_serialization::sbe::error::SbeDecodeError")
    : Common decode errors for malformed or truncated payloads.
*   [`GroupSizeEncoding`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/primitives/struct.GroupSizeEncoding.html "struct nautilus_serialization::sbe::primitives::GroupSizeEncoding")
     and [`GroupSize16Encoding`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/primitives/struct.GroupSize16Encoding.html "struct nautilus_serialization::sbe::primitives::GroupSize16Encoding")
    : Group header decoders.
*   [`decode_var_string8`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/primitives/fn.decode_var_string8.html "fn nautilus_serialization::sbe::primitives::decode_var_string8")
    : varString8 decoder helper.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/index.html#reexports)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use cursor::[SbeCursor](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/cursor/struct.SbeCursor.html "struct nautilus_serialization::sbe::cursor::SbeCursor") ;`

`pub use error::[MAX_GROUP_SIZE](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/error/constant.MAX_GROUP_SIZE.html "constant nautilus_serialization::sbe::error::MAX_GROUP_SIZE") ;`

`pub use error::[SbeDecodeError](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/error/enum.SbeDecodeError.html "enum nautilus_serialization::sbe::error::SbeDecodeError") ;`

`pub use primitives::[GroupSize16Encoding](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/primitives/struct.GroupSize16Encoding.html "struct nautilus_serialization::sbe::primitives::GroupSize16Encoding") ;`

`pub use primitives::[GroupSizeEncoding](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/primitives/struct.GroupSizeEncoding.html "struct nautilus_serialization::sbe::primitives::GroupSizeEncoding") ;`

`pub use primitives::[decode_var_string8](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/primitives/fn.decode_var_string8.html "fn nautilus_serialization::sbe::primitives::decode_var_string8") ;`

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/sbe/index.html#modules)

# nautilus_serialization::msgpack - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/index.html#main-content)

[Module msgpack](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_serialization](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/index.html)

Module msgpack Copy item path
=============================

Expand description

Re-export MsgPack serialization helpers for consumers expecting to configure codecs via this crate. Provides MsgPack serialization support for types implementing \[`Serializable`\].

This module contains traits for MsgPack serialization and deserialization, separated from the core \[`Serializable`\] trait to allow independent opt-in.

Traits[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/index.html#traits)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[FromMsgPack](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/trait.FromMsgPack.html "trait nautilus_serialization::msgpack::FromMsgPack")

Provides deserialization from MsgPack encoded bytes.

[MsgPackSerializable](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/trait.MsgPackSerializable.html "trait nautilus_serialization::msgpack::MsgPackSerializable")

Marker trait combining \[`Serializable`\], [`FromMsgPack`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/trait.FromMsgPack.html "trait nautilus_serialization::msgpack::FromMsgPack")
, and [`ToMsgPack`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/trait.ToMsgPack.html "trait nautilus_serialization::msgpack::ToMsgPack")
.

[ToMsgPack](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_serialization/msgpack/trait.ToMsgPack.html "trait nautilus_serialization::msgpack::ToMsgPack")

Provides serialization to MsgPack encoded bytes.

---

# nautilus_core::time - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/index.html#main-content)

[Module time](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_core](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/index.html)

Module time Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_core/time.rs.html#16-779)

Expand description

The core `AtomicTime` for real-time and static clocks.

This module provides an atomic time abstraction that supports both real-time and static clocks. It ensures thread-safe operations and monotonic time retrieval with nanosecond precision.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/index.html#modes)
Modes
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Real-time mode:** The clock continuously syncs with system wall-clock time (via [`SystemTime::now()`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/time/struct.SystemTime.html#method.now "associated function std::time::SystemTime::now")
    ). To ensure strict monotonic increments across multiple threads, the internal updates use an atomic compare-and-exchange loop (`time_since_epoch`). While this guarantees that every new timestamp is at least one nanosecond greater than the last, it may introduce higher contention if many threads call it heavily.
    
*   **Static mode:** The clock is manually controlled via [`AtomicTime::set_time`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/struct.AtomicTime.html#method.set_time "method nautilus_core::time::AtomicTime::set_time")
     or [`AtomicTime::increment_time`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/struct.AtomicTime.html#method.increment_time "method nautilus_core::time::AtomicTime::increment_time")
    , which can be useful for simulations or backtesting. You can switch modes at runtime using [`AtomicTime::make_realtime`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/struct.AtomicTime.html#method.make_realtime "method nautilus_core::time::AtomicTime::make_realtime")
     or [`AtomicTime::make_static`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/struct.AtomicTime.html#method.make_static "method nautilus_core::time::AtomicTime::make_static")
    . In **static mode**, we use acquire/release semantics so that updates from one thread can be observed by another; however, we do not enforce strict global ordering for manual updates. If you need strong, multi-threaded ordering in **static mode**, you must coordinate higher-level synchronization yourself.
    

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/index.html#structs)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[AtomicTime](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/struct.AtomicTime.html "struct nautilus_core::time::AtomicTime")

Represents an atomic timekeeping structure.

Statics[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/index.html#statics)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ATOMIC\_CLOCK\_REALTIME](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/static.ATOMIC_CLOCK_REALTIME.html "static nautilus_core::time::ATOMIC_CLOCK_REALTIME")

Global atomic time in **real-time mode** for use across the system.

[ATOMIC\_CLOCK\_STATIC](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/static.ATOMIC_CLOCK_STATIC.html "static nautilus_core::time::ATOMIC_CLOCK_STATIC")

Global atomic time in **static mode** for use across the system.

Functions[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/index.html#functions)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[duration\_since\_unix\_epoch](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/fn.duration_since_unix_epoch.html "fn nautilus_core::time::duration_since_unix_epoch")

Returns the duration since the UNIX epoch based on [`SystemTime::now()`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/time/struct.SystemTime.html#method.now "associated function std::time::SystemTime::now")
.

[get\_atomic\_clock\_realtime](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/fn.get_atomic_clock_realtime.html "fn nautilus_core::time::get_atomic_clock_realtime")

Returns a static reference to the global atomic clock in **real-time mode**.

[get\_atomic\_clock\_static](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/fn.get_atomic_clock_static.html "fn nautilus_core::time::get_atomic_clock_static")

Returns a static reference to the global atomic clock in **static mode**.

[nanos\_since\_unix\_epoch](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_core/time/fn.nanos_since_unix_epoch.html "fn nautilus_core::time::nanos_since_unix_epoch")

Returns the current UNIX time in nanoseconds, based on [`SystemTime::now()`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/time/struct.SystemTime.html#method.now "associated function std::time::SystemTime::now")
.

Execution state manager for live trading.

This module provides the execution manager for reconciling execution state between the local cache and connected venues, as well as purging old state during live trading.

# nautilus_network::net - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/index.html#main-content)

[Module net](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/index.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_network](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html)

Module net Copy item path
=========================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_network/net.rs.html#16-62)

Expand description

Network abstractions for dependency injection and testing.

This module provides traits and types that allow our networking components to work with both real networking (`tokio::net`) and simulated networking (`turmoil::net`) through dependency injection.

### [§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/index.html#conditional-compilation)
Conditional Compilation

The module re-exports TCP types that are swapped at compile time:

*   Default builds: `tokio::net::{TcpStream, TcpListener}`
*   Builds with `--features turmoil`: `turmoil::net::{TcpStream, TcpListener}`

This allows production code to be tested with turmoil’s network simulation without runtime overhead or code changes when the feature flag is enabled.

Structs[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/index.html#structs)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[RealTcpConnector](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/struct.RealTcpConnector.html "struct nautilus_network::net::RealTcpConnector")

Production TCP connector.

[TcpListener](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/struct.TcpListener.html "struct nautilus_network::net::TcpListener")

A simulated TCP socket server, listening for connections.

[TcpStream](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/struct.TcpStream.html "struct nautilus_network::net::TcpStream")

A simulated TCP stream between a local and a remote socket.

Traits[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/index.html#traits)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[TcpConnector](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/net/trait.TcpConnector.html "trait nautilus_network::net::TcpConnector")

Trait for network types that can establish TCP connections.

---

# RiskEngine in nautilus_risk::engine - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_risk/engine/struct.RiskEngine.html#main-content)

[RiskEngine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_risk/engine/struct.RiskEngine.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_risk](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_risk/index.html)
::[engine](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_risk/engine/index.html)

Struct RiskEngine Copy item path
================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_risk/engine/mod.rs.html#63-72)

    pub struct RiskEngine {
        pub throttled_submit_order: Throttler<SubmitOrder, Box<dyn Fn(SubmitOrder)>>,
        pub throttled_modify_order: Throttler<ModifyOrder, Box<dyn Fn(ModifyOrder)>>,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

Central risk management engine that validates and controls trading operations.

The `RiskEngine` provides comprehensive pre-trade risk checks including order validation, balance verification, position sizing limits, and trading state management. It acts as a gateway between strategy orders and execution, ensuring all trades comply with defined risk parameters and regulatory constraints.

Enum RotationConfig Copy item path
==================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/config.rs.html#220-240)

    pub enum RotationConfig {
        Size {
            max_size: u64,
        },
        Interval {
            interval_ns: u64,
        },
        ScheduledDates {
            interval_ns: u64,
            schedule_ns: UnixNanos,
        },
        NoRotation,
    }

Expand description

Configuration for file rotation in streaming output.

Variants[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/enum.RotationConfig.html#variants)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/enum.RotationConfig.html#variant.Size)

### Size

Rotate based on file size.

#### Fields

`max_size: [u64](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u64.html) `

Maximum buffer size in bytes before rotation.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/enum.RotationConfig.html#variant.Interval)

### Interval

Rotate based on a time interval.

Interval in nanoseconds.

[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/config/enum.RotationConfig.html#variant.ScheduledDates)

### ScheduledDates

Rotate based on scheduled dates.

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")

 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

Account types such as `CashAccount` and `MarginAccount`.

Re-exports[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/index.html#reexports)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use crate::accounts::any::[AccountAny](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/any/enum.AccountAny.html "enum nautilus_model::accounts::any::AccountAny") ;`

[any](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/any/index.html "mod nautilus_model::accounts::any")

Enum wrapper providing a type-erased view over the various concrete [`Account`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/trait.Account.html "trait nautilus_model::accounts::Account")
 implementations.

[base](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/base/index.html "mod nautilus_model::accounts::base")

Base traits and common types shared by all account implementations.

[cash](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/cash/index.html "mod nautilus_model::accounts::cash")

A cash account that cannot hold leveraged positions.

[margin](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/margin/index.html "mod nautilus_model::accounts::margin")

A margin account capable of holding leveraged positions and tracking instrument-specific leverage ratios.

[stubs](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_model/accounts/stubs/index.html "mod nautilus_model::accounts::stubs")

Lightweight stub implementations useful in unit tests where a full account object is unnecessary.

# nautilus_okx::common - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/index.html#main-content)

[Module common](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_okx](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/index.html)

Module common Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_okx/common/mod.rs.html#16-35)

Expand description

Common functionality shared across the OKX adapter.

This module provides core utilities, constants, and data structures used throughout the OKX integration, including:

*   Authentication credentials and signing.
*   Common enumerations and constants.
*   Parsing utilities for converting OKX data to Nautilus types.
*   URL management for different endpoints.
*   Shared data models.

Modules[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[consts](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/consts/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common::consts")

Core constants shared across the OKX adapter components.

[credential](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/credential/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common::credential")

OKX API credential storage and request signing helpers.

[enums](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/enums/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common::enums")

Enumerations mapping OKX concepts onto idiomatic Nautilus variants.

[models](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/models/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common::models")

Data models representing OKX API payloads consumed by the adapter.

[parse](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/parse/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common::parse")

Parsing utilities that convert OKX payloads into Nautilus domain models.

[urls](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_okx/common/urls/index.html "mod nautilus_okx::common::urls")

URL helpers and endpoint metadata for OKX services.

# nautilus_network::ratelimiter - Rust

[Skip to main content](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/ratelimiter/index.html#main-content)

[Module ratelimiter](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/ratelimiter/index.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[nautilus\_network](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_network/index.html)

Module ratelimiter Copy item path
=================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_network/ratelimiter/mod.rs.html#16-591)

Expand description

A rate limiter implementation heavily inspired by [governor](https://github.com/antifuchs/governor)
.

The governor does not support different quota for different key. It is an open [issue](https://github.com/antifuchs/governor/issues/193)
.

#### pub fn [update\_bar](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/portfolio/struct.Portfolio.html#method.update_bar)
(&mut self, bar: &Bar)

Updates portfolio calculations based on a new bar.

Updates cached bar close prices and recalculates unrealized PnL.

#### pub fn [update\_order](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/portfolio/struct.Portfolio.html#method.update_order)
(&mut self, event: &OrderEventAny)

Updates portfolio calculations based on an order event.

Handles balance updates for order fills and margin calculations for order changes.

#### pub fn [update\_position](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_portfolio/portfolio/struct.Portfolio.html#method.update_position)
(&mut self, event: &PositionEvent)

Updates portfolio calculations based on a position event.

Recalculates net positions, unrealized PnL, and margin requirements.

Struct NautilusKernelBuilder Copy item path
===========================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/builder.rs.html#33-52)

    pub struct NautilusKernelBuilder { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

Builder for constructing a [`NautilusKernel`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/kernel/struct.NautilusKernel.html "struct nautilus_system::kernel::NautilusKernel")
 with a fluent API.

Provides a convenient way to configure and build a kernel instance with optional components and settings.

Implementations[§](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/builder/struct.NautilusKernelBuilder.html#implementations)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/builder.rs.html#54-215)

( name: [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
, trader\_id: TraderId, environment: Environment, ) -> Self

Creates a new [`NautilusKernelBuilder`](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/builder/struct.NautilusKernelBuilder.html "struct nautilus_system::builder::NautilusKernelBuilder")
 with required parameters.

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/builder.rs.html#82-85)

#### pub const fn [with\_instance\_id](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/nautilus_system/builder/struct.NautilusKernelBuilder.html#method.with_instance_id)

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")

Struct StreamingConfig Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://nautilustrader.io/docs/rust-api-latest/src/nautilus_system/config.rs.html#244-255)

    pub struct StreamingConfig {
        pub catalog_path: String,
        pub fs_protocol: String,
        pub flush_interval_ms: u64,
        pub replace_existing: bool,
        pub rotation_config: RotationConfig,
    }

Expand description

Configuration for streaming live or backtest runs to the catalog in feather format.

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")

,

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
